it, not the side with the sword. They knew which side. You did not show them. They
knew. They will walk that side as long as the grief is there. You scratched the ear.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a small body keeping pace with your grief-side.)
The Pup, tonight, knew your step in the dark. You came back from a saving-pause and
they did not bark, did not even raise their head — they thumped their tail once. You
are theirs by step. They are yours by name. The grey kingdom knows you both, now.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a small grey body that names your step without seeing you.)
Tonight, when an enemy lunged, the Pricklehog rolled — small and round, very fast — and
the enemy's grab came up with a spine in the palm. The grab broke. You finished. The
Pricklehog uncurled, satisfied. They will share a spine more often now. You will protect them.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a spine in someone's palm, given by your small kept thing.)
Tonight at Gravewatch's inn the Pricklehog sat on the hearth-stone and the kitchen
warmed around them — a little. They make small hearths. The cook gave them a crust.
You sat closer. You ate. You were a small household around the small fire for one count.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a small hearth made warm by a creature that did not need to.)
Pricklehogs curl up at strange footsteps. The Pricklehog, today, did not curl at yours.
Did not flinch. Walked past your boot, snuffed at it, walked on. You have stopped being
the kind of stranger they hide from. You are now safer in their world. You both are.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a kind of safety in your step a wild thing has decided on.)
Tonight at the saving-place a mouse came near your sleeping head and the Pricklehog —
without rising — clicked their teeth twice. The mouse went. You slept on. You did not
know. You know now. You will be quieter waking, on the small chance the mouse comes back.
Tonight the Magpie landed on your shoulder and, instead of begging, dropped a small
bone button into your cupped palm. They had been carrying it. They had decided you
should have it. The button is old. It is yours now. It matches one of yours. They saw.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a button matched to one of yours by a magpie's careful eye.)
At the bad beat tonight the Magpie cried out — once, sharp, the cry magpies have for
*danger behind you* — and you turned in time. The strike went past you. The Magpie
is exhausted. You fed them, after. They have decided to keep you alive a little longer.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a single warning-cry given to you at the right beat.)
Tonight in Gravewatch the Magpie — for you, you think — took a small ring from a sill.
Brought it to you. You returned it, quietly, to the sill. The Magpie does not understand.
You stroked their head. You did not punish. They will, you think, try again. You will return again.
Tonight at the saving-place the Magpie said — clearly, in your voice — one word. Your
name. The way you would say it if you were greeting yourself. They have been listening
longer than you knew. You said *yes*. They tilted their head. They are very pleased.
Tonight at Gravewatch the Hearth Cat had been at the inn's door, sitting, the whole
afternoon. Innkeeper says. Waiting. When you came in, the Cat did not run. They blinked
at you. Slowly. The Cat is, of all the kept things, the one that has decided you are home.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a Cat that decided you were the one being waited for.)
Tonight at the saving-place the Hearth Cat — who usually keeps two paces from sleeping
people — came up to your chest, where one of your griefs lives, and curled on it. The
grief went a little softer under the warm weight. The Cat purred. You stayed still.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a warm weight on a grief that needed it, given freely.)
Travelling with the Hearth Cat, you have learned to do nothing for an hour at a time.
Not waste an hour. Sit. Watch the light. The Cat models it. You have come round to it.
An hour of doing nothing is, you now understand, one of the things the kingdom needed of you.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — an hour of doing nothing that the Cat has shown you to value.)
The Hearth Cat brought you a mouse this morning. Set it neatly on the doorstep. You
thanked them properly. You buried the mouse. The Cat watched you do it. They were not
offended. They had given a gift. You had received it with the respect of the receiving.
Tonight, in the fight, your hireling stayed at your blind side — the one your sword-
arm has trouble watching. They had been doing this for several fights. You noticed,
tonight, that they had been doing it. They are paid. They had decided to do this anyway.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a blind side watched by paid hands who chose to keep watching.)
Tonight at the saving-place your hireling told you their real name. Not the name you
hired them under. The one their family used. They asked you to use it. You did. You
will. They are not just a hireling anymore. Hirelings do not, properly, tell you this.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a real name given to you by someone you had only hired.)
Tonight your hireling argued a route with you — quietly, but firmly. Their route was
better. You took it. They had been a small-roads scout once, somewhere, before. You
had not asked. They had not said. Their experience is now part of how you travel.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a route taught by experience your hireling had not advertised.)
Tonight at the saving-place your hireling brought their whole kit out — the small
cooking-pot, the tinder, the second blanket — and set it down by yours. As if it were
yours together. You did not say. They did not say. The camp is one camp now, not two.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a kit brought to share by someone who was, last week, hired.)
At the worst of it tonight a heavy strike came for you. Your hireling stepped in and
the strike landed on their shield-arm instead. The arm is fine. The arm will ache.
They did not have to. They had decided, somewhere quietly, to. You will not forget it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a blow taken on your behalf by hands paid for less.)
Tonight between fights your hireling showed you a grip — for the weapon you carry,
the way to hold it that keeps the wrist from bruising on the second swing. A small
thing. A career. You learned it. Your wrist will be steadier. You will fight longer.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a grip taught by someone whose career was the grip.)
Tonight at the saving-place there was a count — a long pause — where your hireling
looked at the gold-line of the count and looked at you and considered whether the pay
was worth it. They stayed. You both knew. They could have gone. They didn't. You felt it.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a count where someone considered leaving you for the money.)
Tonight at the Gravewatch inn your hireling sat at your table and drank a small ale
with you. They had refused, before. Tonight, no. Tonight they sat. You did not toast.
You drank in companionable silence. They went to sleep first. You watched the door for them.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a small ale shared in silence by someone who had refused before.)
Tonight, going through your pack at a zone-arrival, you found a small pendant — wood,
worn smooth, on a leather thong. Your fallen hireling's. They had put it in your pack
in the hour before the bad fight. They had known. The pendant rides with you now.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a pendant put into your pack by hands that knew they were going.)
Tonight in Gravewatch's inn an old fighter at the next table looked at the empty chair
beside you, where your fallen hireling used to sit, and said, quietly, *Ah.* Just *Ah.*
They had known your hireling. They poured a small drink. They left. You sat the count out.
You have learned, since your hireling fell, to eat alone in a manner that is not lonely.
Their absence is a presence now. You leave a little room on the bench. The room is
company. You did not know this could work. It is working. You stand straighter after meals.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a way of eating alone you learned from someone who is not eating with you.)
Tonight you noticed a small dark mark on your belt-buckle that you had not cleaned off.
Your hireling's. From the bad fight. It will not come off — you have tried. It is now
part of the buckle. You will carry the mark for the rest of this run. You can carry it.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a stain that would not lift, kept on the gear of the living.)
Tonight in the Chronicle room at Gravewatch you saw a fresh line — added since you last
looked. Your hireling's name. Their date. *Walked beside [you].* Whoever keeps the
Chronicle had heard. They had added the line for you. You stood at it a long count.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a name in the Chronicle for the one who walked beside you and fell.)
Tonight at the hireling-hall a new one — younger, leaner — came to you and said, *I
knew them.* The fallen one. They had worked one job together, a year before, in a
town you have not been to. They wanted you to know. They sat with you. You both kept the count.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a stranger who knew your dead and came to tell you.)
Tonight, walking — companion at one side, hireling at the other, the pet ahead — you
had the count of a small family. You did not say. None of them said. The kingdom
noted. You stood up taller for the rest of the day. You were not, tonight, alone.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a small family on the road, the once, by your own gathering.)
Tonight at the saving-place the pet — who usually picks you — chose your companion's
knee to lean against. Your companion went still, as if afraid to disturb. You smiled.
Your companion smiled back. The pet has decided about the household. The household is held.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a pet's small choice, in trust, of someone else in your household.)
Tonight at the inn your hireling kept a meal warm under a cloth for your companion, who
was late back. The hireling did not, properly, like your companion. They had decided
to keep the meal anyway. Your household has, between its members, made a small truce.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a meal kept warm by hands that didn't have to.)
Tonight you, your companion, and the pet kept the same silence for a long count of the
walking. Not a tense silence. The good kind. The kind that means everyone is fine. The
pet's ears were forward. Nobody spoke. The road took you all gently. You will keep the silence.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a shared silence that nobody had to break.)
Tonight at the saving-place, looking at your companion, the hireling, the pet — at all
the ones who walk with you now — you understood, fully, that you are the keeper of
this household. The keeping had landed on you. You agreed to it. You set it on yourself.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an agreement to be the keeper of the household you have gathered.)
On your weapon's grip, where the smith's thumb rested while she worked, there is a
small dark smudge of her temper-oil — old, set in. You did not wipe it off. It steadies
your hand against the grip in a way you can feel. The smith's thumbprint, kept.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the smith's thumb, kept on your weapon's grip.)
Today the smith handed your weapon back to you after the upgrade and — between her
hands and yours — she held it one count too long. As if reluctant. As if the work she
had done was for someone she wanted to send out armoured. You thanked her quietly.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a weapon handed back with one count of held intent.)
Today the smith, polishing your weapon between strikes of the hammer, called it by a
name. Not the name you had given it. Not yours. A woman's name. The smith blushed when
she noticed you had heard. She did not explain. The weapon answers to both names now.
Walking through the smith's yard today, a single spark from the forge flew up and out
the door past your ear. Warm. Brief. Yours, the smith said, smiling at the air. You
shook your head. She shrugged. The forge has its preferences. The spark was yours.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a spark from a forge that decided about you.)
Today the smith — between the work — taught you, with a small gesture, to lay your
weapon down at the door when you come into a house. The kingdom does not, anymore.
She does. You will, now. It is a small dignity. The dignity will steady your shoulders.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a manner taught by the smith that will steady you in doorways.)
Today, going past the smith's, you saw — high on a shelf — the handle she had stripped
from your weapon when she upgraded it. She had kept the wood. You did not ask why.
She had kept it for you, in case you wanted it. You did not ask for it back. It stays there.
Tonight, in the fight, your weapon swung true on a strike that should have gone wide.
Out of the strike came, faintly, a sound — the ring of the smith's hammer on the anvil.
She had laid a small blessing on the weapon. The strike landed. The blessing was real.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a smith's blessing kept in the weapon, spent on one good strike.)
The smith, today, asked for the weapon back briefly — to mark the pommel with the small
Gravewatch stamp that says *I made this.* It is on the pommel now. Older fighters will
see it. They will know. They will not test you. The stamp will travel with you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Gravewatch stamp on your pommel, recognized by those who know.)
At the worst beat tonight the weapon, in your hand, tightened — as if for one count
it had been held by another hand that had held it harder. The smith had had it. So had
the one before her. Each of them, in the bad beat, made room for you. You stood.
Tonight after the fight, you cleaned the weapon the way the smith had shown you —
twice on each side, oil from the wrist of your other hand, breath blown along the edge.
Slower than you used to. Better. Your weapon will dull less. Your hand will be steadier.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a cleaning-ritual the smith taught you that you now keep.)
On the smith's bench today there was a long blade — too long for you, made for someone
taller. The smith said it had been there a year. Said somebody had ordered it, never
come back. Said the order had been for you. You did not take it. You stood by it a count.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a blade you did not take, made for the size of you, once.)
Today the smith — who had been simply *the smith* the whole run — told you her name.
Halna's eldest daughter. You did not know Halna had had a daughter who survived. The
smith had survived. The smith has a name. You said the name back. She nodded once.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a smith's name, given to you, made survivor's name.)
The armor you bought has, between the last zone and this one, finally settled. The
buckle has stopped digging. The pad has shaped to your ribs. You forget you are wearing
it now, except in the moments when it stops a blow. From now on, you are the armor.
(+1 Defense, permanently — armor that has finally shaped to the body wearing it.)
There is old blood in the lining of the armor — not yours, not the last fight's. Older.
The quartermaster told you. You did not mind. Old blood does not, in the kingdom,
infect; it knows where it stood. The blood holds the armor's shape. The shape is yours now.
In the worst beat tonight a strap on your armor came loose — and the enemy's grab,
going for the strap, came up with nothing in their fist. You hadn't loosened the strap.
The armor had. You re-tightened it after, slow. The armor is keeping you on purpose.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a strap that loosened, on purpose, in your favour.)
Today at the quartermaster's, with the new piece on you, she pressed a small wooden
token into your palm — the Gravewatch quartermaster's mark, the one given to fighters
who survive long enough to need a third piece. You did not need three. You will have it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a quartermaster's token, given to you for the surviving.)
Tonight the padding of your armor, for one count, smelled — distinctly — of a kitchen.
Onions, broth, the smoke of an oven. You don't know whose kitchen the padding had been
near. You stood still a count, breathing it. The smell went. The armor breathed normally.
Today, walking Gravewatch in your armor, a widow on the second bench looked at the
buckle — looked at it specifically — and her face went briefly soft. The buckle had
been her husband's. She did not say. You did not ask. You nodded to her. She nodded back.
Tonight, kneeling at a grave to read the name, your chest-piece knocked against the
marker — a small, hollow sound. The marker took the knock. The chest-piece took the
knock back. Some of the dead the marker holds came, very briefly, into the armor. You stood up.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a knock against a grave-marker that travelled both ways.)
Tonight, taking the armor off at the saving-place, you understood that it will outlast
you. The next one to wear it will not be you. You said this — without speaking, only
thinking it — to the armor. The armor agreed. You both came to terms. You slept easier.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a peace made with a piece of gear that will see you out.)
There is a dent in your chest-piece this morning that was not there at last save. Not
a fight-dent. A pressing — as if a heavy thoughtful hand had laid itself on the armor
in the night. You found no scratch around the dent. You will leave it. The armor knows.
Wearing the armor, you have had to learn to walk quieter — the way that does not jingle.
Between the last zone and this, the walking has become natural. Your boots and your
buckles have made their peace with each other. You can sneak when you need to now.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a quieter walk that the armor and you negotiated.)
In the colder air tonight your armor — surprisingly — kept you warm. The metal should
have leached the heat. It did the opposite. Something in the padding has decided to
hold your warmth back for you. The cold zones will be a little kinder now.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — armor that held your warmth back for you in a cold place.)
Tonight at the saving-place you took the armor off and set it down and, for one count,
it kept the shape of someone you used to know. Not yours. Briefly theirs. The armor
knew them. They were a long way away. They were thinking of you. The armor showed you.
Your weapon, tonight in the fight, recalled — quickly, only as the body knows things —
the angle of a strike you had made with it three fights ago. The same strike. You
did not consciously repeat. Your hand obeyed the weapon. The strike landed perfectly.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a weapon-memory of a strike, returned to your hand.)
After the fight tonight your weapon's grip stayed warm in your hand longer than it
should have — the heat of you having held it long enough that the wood had remembered.
You will not need to warm your hand on it before next fight. The wood is ready.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a grip that does not need warming, having warmed to you.)
Tonight you understood, with the weapon in your hand after the kill, that the weapon
will not break in your hand. It will break — eventually, it always breaks — in someone
else's. The next person to hold it. That is its decision. It is your decision too. You agreed.
After tonight's fight a drop of someone else's blood — small, dark — soaked into the
wood of your weapon, against your palm. You cannot get it out. You stopped trying.
The drop is in the wood now. The wood is also in your hand. Some of the drop is in you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a drop of someone else's blood that travelled through your weapon.)
The weapon, tonight, swung one full count easier than it had at the start of the run.
Not lighter — easier. The arm has agreed with it. The arm and the weapon are one
machine now. You can hit harder, faster. You will hit faster than you used to.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a weapon and an arm that have agreed to be one machine.)
Mid-fight tonight, between strikes, you turned your weapon — for one count — pommel-
first toward a friend at your side. Not aiming. Marking. The kingdom's old fighters do
this to signal *I have not forgotten you in the kill.* Your friend saw. They nodded.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an old fighter's signal, given between strikes.)
On contact with another blade tonight your weapon sang — low, briefly, a sustained
note. Not the usual ring. A note. The note had a shape. It was, you think, the first
note of a song. The other blade went silent. The weapon will not sing twice. It did, once.
Tonight your weapon — without your noticing — picked up the third line of a song you
had been humming on the climb. The line is in the steel now. It is going to the
Summit with you. The Summit will hear it from your weapon, when the time comes. You will sing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a song-line picked up by your weapon, kept for the Summit.)
The handle of your weapon, between the last fight and tonight, has smoothed itself in
the exact place your thumb rests. The wood and your thumb have come to an agreement.
You can grip longer without tiring. The grip is, now, the shape of the work it does.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a handle that has smoothed itself to the shape of your thumb.)
Tonight in the fight your weapon, between two swings, held back from striking — the
enemy was the same kind it had killed many of before. The weapon paused. You took the
next swing. The weapon went again. You both, for one count, considered the kindness of pause.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a weapon that paused for one count in respect of those it had killed.)
Tonight you walked with a trophy in your pocket — feather, tooth, scale, whatever you
carry — and the pocket, against your thigh, warmed. The trophy is keeping you company.
Trophies do this for those who keep them. You will keep them more carefully from now on.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a trophy warming the pocket of one who has kept it well.)
Today the Beastmaster, as you passed the pen, lifted their head — sniffed once — and
named the animal whose trophy you were carrying. Not by looking. By the smell on you.
They tipped their head, approving. You walked on. The smell is yours. The Beastmaster knows.
Tonight, taking a trophy from a kill, you knelt — without thinking — and made the cut
slow. The kingdom's old hunters used to do this. You have not been told. You did it
anyway. The trophy is cleaner. The taking is, for now, an honest taking.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a kneeling for the trophy that the body has chosen for you.)
Today, reaching into your pocket for a coin, your finger was held — for one count —
by the curled paw of a small trophy. As if the paw had decided. Then it let go. You
took the coin. You did not look at the paw. You walked on. The paw will hold you again.
Today at the Beastmaster's there was a small leather collar on the high peg — your size,
old, the kind that has been on a dog before. The Beastmaster said it had been waiting.
Said you would know what to do with it. You took it. You don't, yet. You will.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a collar set aside by the Beastmaster for hands you have not yet earned.)
Tonight, turning a trophy over in your hand, you said — without thinking — the name
of a friend who has been dead. The trophy, in the kingdom's old way, has lent itself
to the naming. You are, briefly, in the room with them. You stood up after. You stood taller.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a trophy that let you name a friend who could not be named otherwise.)
Tonight a stranger came to you and handed back a trophy you had dropped — three zones
ago, in the bad fight. They had carried it that whole way to find you. They would not
take payment. They were the friend of someone you had laid to rest. They went on their road.
Today at the Beastmaster's pen they told you the old name — the kingdom-old name — of
one of the creatures you have been hunting. They said the name made the killing harder.
You said *yes.* You took the name. Your hand will be slower next time. Your hand will be more deliberate.
(+1 Attack, permanently — an old name for a creature, taken to make your hand deliberate.)
Today at Gravewatch's shop the keeper, behind the counter, took out — from a low shelf
— a small loaf and pushed it across to you without charging. *For the road.* You took
it. You did not protest. You eat better on the road for it. You will eat better tonight.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a loaf set aside for you by hands that did not charge for it.)
Tonight, drinking the last of one of your potions between fights, it tasted — briefly —
of the herb-garden you played in as a child. You don't remember the garden. You
remember the taste. The garden is in the potion. You will keep some of the taste in you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a potion that tasted of a garden you had forgotten.)
Today at Gravewatch's shop the keeper, paying you out for something you had brought,
said your name aloud — for the first time you had heard from them. Quietly. Like a
person testing the weight of a friend's word. You said theirs back. Equal weight.
(+1 Defense, permanently — your name said aloud by a shopkeeper testing the weight of it.)
Tonight, walking, you noticed that your potion pouch — the one with the small things
you carry — was warm against your thigh. Not the warmth of body-heat. The warmth of
things that have decided to be ready. They are ready. You walked easier knowing.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a pouch of consumables that has decided to stand ready.)
Today at the shop an old one — leaning against the counter, between purchases — gave
you a small coin in exchange for a story about where you had been. You told them. Not
the worst parts. The next parts. They paid in full. They were the last to ask of you tonight.
Tonight in the bad beat you drank a potion and it caught — went down rough, the way
potions do when the bottle has, somewhere along the way, been knocked. The healing came.
The next breath was harder. Your chest will be a little tireder, from now on, after potions.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a potion that went down rough and took some of your breath.)
Travelling through Gravewatch you have learned, slowly, to count the change carefully
before pocketing — the way merchants in the kingdom expect of careful customers. You
are not a soft mark anymore. The shopkeepers tip their heads at you in the new way.
Today you bought a small consumable — a sweet, a pastry, something that does not heal
— and gave it, without speaking, to a child in Gravewatch's square. The child did not
thank you in words. They opened it. They ate. The square went on its day. You walked on lighter.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a thing bought for the eating of a child.)
Tonight in Gravewatch, going home through the empty square, the air at your door —
the door to where you sleep — was, for one count, quieter than the air around it. The
Pall stood there. It did not enter. It noted the door. It went. The door is marked now.
Tonight, looking down at a patch of dusty path, you saw a small print — not a foot, not
a hoof, not a hand. The shape of an absence. A spot where the dust had been pressed by
something that does not, properly, weigh anything. The Pall passed here. It saw you.
Tonight, walking, you felt — for one count — a coldness at the back of your collar. As
if a thing without a face had breathed onto your neck. You turned. Nothing. You went on.
Your collar will be a little cooler from now on. A small thread of warmth has left you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a breath into your collar by a thing without a face.)
Tonight, between zones, you heard your name called — quietly, in nobody's voice. The
voice was not unkind. It was practising. The Pall is learning to call you. You did not
answer. The call faded. The Pall will call again. You will, eventually, not answer.
Tonight in the dark of a walking-stretch your pack shifted, briefly, as if a hand had
brushed it. Nothing fell out. Nothing was taken. But the Pall has touched something of yours.
Tonight at the inn in Gravewatch a chair at your table — the one across from you —
moved out, faintly, between counts. As if someone were sitting. You did not sit across
from yourself in the chair. Something else, briefly, did. The chair settled. You ate.
Tonight, going to sleep, you took stock. The Pall had been near you today. It had not
taken anything. Not a memory. Not a small habit. Not a name. Today the Pall, for
whatever reason, let you keep what was yours. You felt the keeping. You stood up warmer.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a day the Pall chose, for once, not to take anything.)
Tonight, crossing a particular doorstep in Gravewatch, your boot — for one count — left
a faint dark mark. Like an ink-print. It faded. The Pall has begun to log where you
have stepped. The Pall is keeping records. Records are how it begins. You stepped on.
For one count tonight the air around you — the air you were breathing — was held by
the Pall. Not bad air. Held air. As if the Pall were taking a sample of you. It let
the air go after. You breathed again. The next breath was the same air. It went down differently.
Tonight, in your purse, one of your coins had — between morning and evening — changed.
Same weight. Same gold. The face on it was now slightly different. A face the Pall is trying to remember. You closed the purse.
Tonight at the saving-place you woke once, very briefly, and the air at the window had
the shape of an attention turned on you. Not eyes. An attention. The Pall had been
watching you sleep. You closed your eyes. The attention waited. Eventually it went.
Tonight at the saving you understood — for one count — that the Pall keeps a daily
account of you. Not malicious. Clerkly. The day's small choices, the kindnesses, the
small unkindnesses, all tallied. You stood straighter knowing. The Pall is taking notes.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an awareness that you are watched and accounted for daily.)
Tonight, mid-walk, you blinked. In the small count of darkness behind your closed eyes,
the Pall did not also close its eyes. It was watching the dark inside your eyelid. You
opened your eyes. The Pall went. Your lid will be a little tireder from now on.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a blink the Pall did not blink with you in.)
At the worst beat tonight the Pall — without your turning — studied one of your scars.
The small one. The one you got before the kingdom. The one you do not, ordinarily,
think of. The Pall now has the scar in its inventory. The scar is — slightly — known.
Today, mid-walk, you had a thought — about your mother, about a particular morning
with her. The Pall followed the thought. You felt the following. The thought was
complete by the time it was finished. The Pall watched it complete. It did not, today, take it.
Tonight at the inn the Pall watched you eat. The whole meal. Not impatient. Not greedy.
Curious. As if it had not, in a long time, seen anybody eat. You ate slowly. The Pall
watched the whole way to the bottom of the bowl. Then it went. You did not lose your appetite.
Today, a small kindness you did — without naming it as one, hardly noticing — was
logged by the Pall. It surprised the Pall. It surprised you. The Pall kept the log of
the kindness with — you suspect — a kind of confusion. You stood up taller for the log.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a small kindness of yours that surprised even the Pall into logging it.)
Today you knelt at a grave. The Pall, watching, did not interrupt. Did not approach.
Held a respectful distance. The Pall, you understand now, is not all cruelty. It has,
buried somewhere in it, the manners of the kingdom it was. It kept the manners. You stood up.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a moment the Pall remembered its manners watching you.)
Tonight the Pall studied your companion — not you, your companion. Took a kind of
interest. Your companion did not feel it. You did. The Pall has filed your companion
away as a thing of interest. You will watch the Pall's interest in your companion now.
Tonight in a lit street the Pall, for one count, followed your shadow rather than you —
the lantern-cast shadow walking ahead. The shadow did not notice. You did. The Pall
is making an inventory of the parts of you. The shadow has been inventoried. So have you.
Today, mid-sentence, you reached for a small word — a daily word, *however*, *anyway*,
you cannot now say which — and it was not there. The Pall has it. The Pall took it
gently. The sentence finished without it. You will say sentences with one fewer word now.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a small daily word taken cleanly by the Pall.)
Today the Pall took the smell of a particular kitchen — your aunt's, the one with the
low ceiling, where you ate as a child. You can describe the kitchen. You cannot smell
it. The smell is gone. The Pall has it. The Pall has many of these. You stood up emptier.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a kitchen-smell the Pall took out of your memory cleanly.)
At the worst beat tonight the Pall — quietly, between two of yours — took a breath out
of you. Mid-fight. Just one. You survived. You almost did not. From now on, when the
fight is bad, one of your breaths will be short. The Pall has it. The Pall keeps such things.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a breath taken from you mid-fight by the Pall.)
Tonight you hummed — under your breath, walking — a song you have always known. You
got to the chorus. The song's name was gone. You can still hum. The name has left you.
The Pall has it. You will go on humming. The song will go on. The name will not come back.
Tonight you remembered, dimly, that someone in Gravewatch did a kindness for you at
the start of this run. You cannot recall what. You cannot recall who. The Pall has
taken the kindness. The kindness is gone from you. The kindness was done. The kindness is gone.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a kindness done for you, taken by the Pall as if to file away.)
Tonight, trying to recall the face of one of your teachers — the kind one, the one who
made you the kind of person who could be made into something — you could not. The
Pall has the face. You can write the name. You cannot see the face. You will not, again.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a teacher's face taken by the Pall, kept by the Pall.)
Tonight, signing a small note at the saving-place, your handwriting — for one letter —
was not your handwriting. The letter sat there in someone else's hand. You did not
rewrite. The Pall is collecting samples of you. The signature is now slightly the Pall's.
Tonight, in the dark before sleep, you reached for the memory of a first kiss — yours,
the very first, the one that made you understand other people existed. It was not
there. The Pall has taken it. The kingdom keeps the kiss now. You will not get it back.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — a first kiss taken by the Pall to keep for the kingdom.)
Today, mid-walk, the Pall — without your noticing — took one of your steps. A small
one. Now you walk on the kingdom's slightly. You walk on yours mostly. The mix is hard
to feel until you are tired. Then you feel it. You will be tired sooner from now on.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a step of yours that is now, partly, the Pall's.)
Tonight you reached for the taste of a winter apple — the cold-storage ones, the kind
your grandmother kept in the bin under the stairs. The taste was not there. The Pall has the apple.
Tonight the Pall reached for the first song you ever learned — the one your mother
sang while she cleaned, the one without any words anymore, the one that is just a
shape. The Pall reached. The Pall withdrew. The song is yours. The Pall could have. It did not.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a song the Pall could have taken and chose, today, not to.)
Tonight at the saving-place, in the dream just before waking, the Pall walked through
and left a face you love alone. Touched everything else. Did not touch the face. You
woke able to picture the face. The Pall could have taken it. The Pall, today, did not.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a face the Pall left alone in your dreaming.)
Tonight, sitting somewhere quiet, the Pall let you have one room from your childhood —
whole, fully — for a count. The brown wallpaper. The smell of paper and dust. The
window. You sat in the room a count. The Pall withdrew. The room is yours, in your head.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a room of your childhood kept whole, today, by the Pall.)
Tonight the Pall — quietly, somewhere — sang one line of a song it had taken from you,
weeks ago. The line came back into your air. The Pall sang it. You knew the line. The
Pall was, briefly, apologising. The Pall does not, often. You took the line back, briefly.
Today, on the path, the smell of bread baking — a smell the Pall had taken from you a
while ago — came back. Not the kitchen. Just the smell. The Pall, for one count, let
the smell rest in your nose again. Then withdrew it. But you smelled it. You smelled.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a smell handed back briefly by the Pall in passing.)
Today the Pall reached toward your companion. Toward the name your companion had told
you the night before. The Pall could have. The Pall, instead, withdrew. The Pall, you
think, has decided about your companion in some specific way. The name is yours together.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a companion's name spared, today, by the Pall.)
Tonight you walked into a room that should have been cold — abandoned, unheated — and
the room was warm. Not from a fire. From an attention. The Pall had decided to warm
the room for you, for one count. You stood in the warm air. You did not thank it. The Pall went.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a warming of cold air done by the Pall as a small mercy.)
Tonight, mid-thought, a word came back to you — a word the Pall had taken weeks ago,
the small daily word that left a hole in your sentences. The word is back. The Pall
returned it. The Pall returned a thing once it had taken. You will use the word more carefully.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a word the Pall took and decided, today, to give back.)
Tonight you came late to a door in Gravewatch and it was open. It should not have
been. The keeper would have locked it at this hour. The Pall had kept it open. You
went in. The Pall, briefly, was a kind landlord. The Pall closed the door behind you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a door kept unlocked by the Pall against the hour you would arrive.)
Tonight at the inn, one of your meals — the bread, you think — was blessed. Faintly.
The way the kingdom's old priests used to bless bread, before the rite. The Pall did
it. The Pall remembered, briefly, what it had been before. The blessing held all night.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a meal blessed by the Pall in a remembering of who it had been.)
Tonight the Pall, walking with you a while, began to teach you — without speaking, the
way the Pall teaches — how to keep a forgetting. The mechanics of it. The way the rite
shapes a kingdom's silences. You did not protest. You listened. You will need to know.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the start of a lesson the Pall is teaching its successor.)
Tonight on the road the Pall — for the first time — matched its step to yours. Not
behind you. Beside. Walking with you. The kingdom would say this is how the Pall
courts a successor. You walked at the same pace. The Pall and you, today, were a pair.
Tonight you had a long grief to sit with. The Pall — which has, lately, watched all
your grieving — withdrew. Stood at the edge of the field of the grief. Let you have it
alone. As if to say, *this one is yours.* You sat the count out. You stood up taller.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a grief the Pall had the decency to leave you alone with.)
Tonight, in your sleep — or in the place between — the Pall introduced you to the
Summit wind. Like an older one introducing a younger one to a thing they will share.
The wind acknowledged you. You acknowledged the wind. The Pall stood by. The introduction was real.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an introduction made by the Pall between you and the Summit wind.)
Tonight at the saving-place the Pall, quietly, told you the name of the climber it had
been before — the one who, generations ago, made the original agreement. You knew the
name. The kingdom had had it. You will not say it now. You will say it at the Summit.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a name from the Pall, kept in you for the moment of arrival.)
Tonight for one count the Pall let you breathe its air — the air the Pall lives in.
Cold. Old. Steady. Your lungs did not rebel. They handled the air. The Pall let the
breath out of you. Your lungs are now able for the air. The Summit will not stagger you.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a lungful of the Pall's own air, taken and let go.)
Tonight, going to sleep, you took stock. The Pall had been kind to you today. Several
small kindnesses. You did not used to think of the Pall as kind. Tonight you do. The
Pall is preparing you. Part of preparing you is, today, kindness. You can carry it.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a day on which the Pall was kind to you, deliberately.)
Tonight, in the dark before sleep, the Pall — for one count — showed you its face. You
did not recognize it. You almost did. The face will come into focus more, as you climb.
By the Summit you will know. You did not look away. The Pall did not, either. You both kept the count.
Tonight at the saving you understood — clearly, without speaking — that the Pall has
set aside a place for you. At the top. A chair. The seat is being prepared. The chair
knows your shape already. You did not protest. You walked the next zone steadier.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a chair set aside at the Summit by the Pall for you.)
Tonight the Pall — briefly, between one zone and the next — gave you one of its
burdens to carry. The weight of a kingdom's small daily forgetting. You carried it. It
was heavy. You stood up under it. The Pall took it back. You are ready. The Pall knows.
(+3 Max HP, permanently — a Pall-burden carried briefly by you, proving the keeper.)
Tonight you stooped to a niche in a wall — the kind only you would think to look in —
and found a small knife. The handle was wrapped in the way you wrap handles. A younger
you put it there, for someone. The someone is you. The knife fits your hand exactly.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a knife left by an earlier you, found tonight by this you.)
Today on a path you found a small leather pouch — worn smooth, the strap stitched in a
particular pattern. The pattern is your stitching. The initials inside are yours. Some
previous you dropped this here, hoping. You took it. The pouch is back with you again.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a pouch you stitched in a previous life, recovered.)
Tonight, in a page you found, a note was pinned at the margin in your handwriting. The
note was a single sentence: *go to the second altar, kneel slowly.* You did not know
what the sentence meant. Some other you did. You will, when the time comes, know.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a sentence written by a previous you for this you to find.)
Tonight you knelt at a grave — a particular one, in Gravewatch's small yard — and the
stone, under your palm, was warm. As if the one inside had been waiting. You read the
name. The name was yours. The name was an earlier yours. The warmth was the recognition.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a grave that warmed for you because you, once, were inside.)
Tonight you stooped to pick up a button on the road. Bone, scored once. The other half
of the pair on your coat. Your coat. The same coat. A previous you wore it. A previous
you dropped the button. The button has come home. You sewed it back on at the inn.
Mid-fight tonight your weapon, for one count, sat differently in your hand — as if a
different palm than yours had held it last. Not different. Earlier. A previous you. The
grip steadied. The strike landed. The blade remembered being held by someone like you.
Tonight in Gravewatch's shop the keeper unlocked a low drawer and took out a potion
they had been holding. A previous you had left it on credit, never come back to claim
it. The keeper had kept it. They gave it to you. *Yours,* they said. You took it. It was.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a potion paid for by a previous you, held against your return.)
Tonight in the Witherwood you came to a tree with a carving — a name, your name, cut
in a hand younger than yours but recognizable. A previous you carved it. The wood has
grown around the cut. The name is permanent now. The kingdom keeps it for the next you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a name a younger you carved that has grown into the wood.)
The roads of Mournhold know your step — not from this run, from before. They know how
you set your boot. They give you, today, the smoother edges. Older travellers do not
get this. Older travellers have not been you before. The kingdom recognizes the foot.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a road that has been walked by your previous feet.)
Tonight at Gravewatch's inn the innkeeper, without saying, handed you a small key. *He
left it for the next you,* she said. The box it opens is in your old room. Inside: a
small jet earring, a folded letter, two coppers. You read the letter. It said nothing.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a box at the inn that a previous you left for this you.)
Tonight on the road you saw a Hollowed at a distance. The shape of it — the lean, the
shoulder, the way it set its head — was your shape. A previous you, risen, walking. You
did not approach. It did not approach. You went around. The kingdom is full of you now.
Tonight a Hollowed — at the edge of the path, in dim light — recognized your walk. The
Hollowed had been you. The Hollowed did not attack. It tilted its head. Considered. Then
stepped back. Some courtesy survives in the kingdom of the dead. You walked past whole.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Hollowed that had been you and granted you safe passage.)
Tonight in the fight you killed a Hollowed and, at the killing stroke, recognized the
face — yours. An earlier yours. You finished it. You had to. The kingdom does not let
the unresolved rest until they are put down. You laid the body out. You said the name.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — a Hollowed that had been you, put down by you.)
Tonight you laid one of your own Hollowed to rest properly. You had to. You did it
properly. The kingdom's old way — palms folded, the small words, the eye closed. The
version of you in the Hollowed body went quiet. It thanked you, soundlessly. You stood up.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a self of yours laid properly to rest by the next self.)
When the Hollowed went down tonight, in its hand was a small object — a worn token,
the kind a previous you might have carried. It was. The Hollowed had been holding it
for you. Your hand closed around it. The token is yours again. You will not lose it.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a token kept by your own Hollow until you came to take it.)
Tonight two Hollowed passed you on the road. Both had been you. They walked together.
They nodded as you went by. Not hostile. Not friendly. The way old comrades nod when
the next watch passes. You nodded back. You are a small order in the kingdom now.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a recognition from two of your own past selves.)
Today in the forest you found a message — carved into a trunk, in the hand of a
previous you. The carving was rough, the hand half-gone, but the words were clear:
*don't trust the second emissary at the Border.* You will remember. Your hand will too.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a warning a Hollow of yours carved for the next you.)
Tonight at Atrél's altar there was a Hollowed kneeling — yours, by the shoulders. You
did not interrupt. You knelt at the other side. Atrél stood near both of you. The
Hollowed left without speaking. Atrél placed a hand, briefly, on your head. You went on.
Tonight you apologised to a Hollowed of yours. For the climb you didn't finish, the
fights you fled, the choices you made that put them in the ground. The Hollowed
listened. The Hollowed did not, properly, accept. The Hollowed did not reject either.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — an apology owed to one of your past selves, delivered.)
Tonight at the saving-place a Hollowed of yours came to the edge of the lamp-light and
— for one count — held your hand. Briefly. Without pressure. As an older sibling would
hold a younger's hand at a wake. Then it went back into the dark. You slept warmer.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a hand-hold from one of your own past selves.)
Tonight in the Chronicle room you found the page of a previous you. You read the whole
line — short, dry, the kingdom's clerk-style. *Fell in the Sodden Reach. Did not return.
Their pack was returned to the Crossroads.* You knew the line. You stood up under it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Chronicle line about a previous you, read in full.)
Tonight in the Chronicle you found, in the margin of a previous you's page, a small
note in another hand: *don't repeat the third turn of the Cantor fight.* The clerk had
been kind. The note has been waiting for you. You will not. Your hand has been warned.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a margin-warning from the Chronicle clerk to your next self.)
Tonight in the Chronicle you went back to a previous you's page and the handwriting,
today, was slightly different. Smaller. Tireder. The clerk recopies, sometimes — when
the ink fades. The clerk recopied today. The ink, in this version, is more tired. You stood by it.
Tonight in the Chronicle the cause-of-death line for one of your previous you was
blank. Not even *unknown.* Blank. The clerk had not been able to find a way to say it.
You did not press. You closed the book. You walked out a little quieter than you came.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a previous you whose end the Chronicle could not record.)
Tonight the Chronicle clerk — old, dry, kindless to most — looked up at you when you
came in and her face did the small thing it does when she recognises a returning
climber. She has known many of you. She has known you. You both nodded. She turned a page for you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — recognition from the Chronicle clerk who has known many of you.)
Tonight tucked into a previous you's Chronicle page was a small folded drawing — a
rough sketch, in pencil. You drew it. You don't remember drawing it. It is a hand.
Yours? Someone else's. It is the kind of drawing one makes for a person one cannot say it to.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a drawing a previous you made and could not give to anyone but the page.)
Tonight you flipped the Chronicle to where this you's page would, eventually, be. The
page was blank. The kingdom is not yet sure of you. You closed the book before the
page could begin to fill itself. You will, you decided, give it a good line. You promised.
Tonight in the Chronicle, on a previous you's page, you saw — for the first time — a
second name. The companion that walked with that you. Their name was in the page too.
You said both names aloud. The page warmed faintly. The Chronicle kept the pair together.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Chronicle page that held you and your companion as a pair.)
Tonight at the Crossroads-marker Chronicle book, the *first line on the first page* —
the one every new character reads — was in your handwriting. You had written it for
yourself, long ago. You read it tonight. You finally understood what it had meant.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Chronicle's first line is yours; you wrote it for the next you.)
Tonight the Chronicle clerk — without saying — tore out a single blank page and gave
it to you. *For when you need to.* She did not say what. You folded it into your coat.
You will write on it when the time comes. The kingdom has set aside a page for your hand.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a blank Chronicle page given to you by hands that know your run.)
Tonight the smith, taking your weapon, did not need to ask which grip you preferred.
She knew. A previous you had told her, years ago. She set the angle. She remembered
for you. Your weapon will swing the right way from now on without your asking.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a smith who knew your grip from a previous life of yours.)
Tonight the innkeeper, without asking, gave you the same key she gave a previous you.
The room is unchanged. Your bed is on the side of the room you used to take. Your hook
is on the left of the door, the way you set it before. The inn kept the shape of you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a room kept ready by an innkeeper for the shape of you.)
Tonight at the Beastmaster's pen one of the pets — a small one, scarred along the eye —
saw you across the yard and came running. It had been a previous you's. It still
remembered. The Beastmaster watched without comment. You knelt. The pet pressed its head.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a pet that remembered you from a previous life and ran to you.)
Tonight in Gravewatch's square a widow, looking up from her bench, called you — for one
count — by the name of a previous you. The name a previous you had asked her to call
them. She corrected herself. She did not need to. You waved. You went on. You felt the keeping.
Tonight at Atrél's altar Atrél, without your speaking, brought out a small folded grief
a previous you had set aside at this altar before falling. Atrél had kept it. You took
it back. You will carry it for the rest of this run, or set it down again, your choice.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a grief returned to you by Atrél after a previous life of yours.)
Tonight at the hireling hall there was an older one — scarred, lean, the kind who has
outlived their employers. They walked with a previous you to the Drowned Holds. They
lived. They remember. They lowered their rate when they saw you. You hired them.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a hireling who walked with a previous you and lowered their rate for this you.)
Tonight Halna, at her stall, had a particular cut of meat held back behind the counter
— the one a previous you had ordered. She had been keeping it. *I knew you'd come,*
she said. She did not charge. You ate it that evening. You ate well. You slept full.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a cut of meat kept by Halna for whoever you turned out to be.)
Tonight Cael, through the stone, called you by a name you had used in a previous life
— before you came back. Cael had not been told. The stone knew. You answered to the
name. Cael said it was correct. The stone keeps such things. You stood up steadier.
(+1 Defense, permanently — Cael saying through the stone the name you used as a previous you.)
Tonight at the Crossroads the lampkeeper had set out one extra lamp. *For when you go
back,* she said. She had been doing this for previous yous. Several. You walked back
through the lit path. The lamps shone for you. You stand straighter in their light from now on.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — extra lamps set out by the keeper for your walk back.)
Tonight at Helka's bench by the Reach the wood — between the last visit and this one —
had been moved to your sitting-height. Higher than she sets it for most. A previous you
had sat there often. She remembered. She set the bench to your length. You sat at home.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a bench set to the height of you by hands that remembered.)
Tonight against an enemy you had never seen, you knew — exactly — when their first
swing would come. You blocked it without thinking. The body knew. A previous you had
blocked the same swing, on a different night, and the body had remembered the count.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a body that knows a swing it did not, this run, see come.)
Tonight in the bad beat your hand went into your pouch and came up with the exact right
potion — not the one nearest, not the one you thought you wanted. The right one. The
previous you that died had wished, in the dying, that they had taken that one. They got their wish.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a potion chosen by a hand that has died wishing for it before.)
Tonight at a branching you came to the path a previous you took — the one that ended
in the fall — and you stopped. Your body would not let you take it again. You took the
other path. The other path was the right path. You did not know. The body knew.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a route refused by a body that remembered taking it badly.)
Tonight you drew steel one count earlier than a previous you would have, against the
same enemy. The count made the difference. The enemy went down. The previous you had
drawn at the second count, and had died of the wait. The wait will not repeat.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a count of draw learned by the body across runs.)
Tonight you entered a room and, before the room knew you were there, you said a word —
quiet, a greeting, the kind of word the kingdom uses with the rooms it has not seen in
a long count. The room recognized the word from a previous you. The room let you in.
Tonight you walked past a small bright object on the path — the kind that catches the
eye. A previous you picked one up here. It was a trap. You knew not to. You walked on.
The body remembered the bite. You did not get bitten. The trap will be on the path still.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a small bright thing left alone because a previous you was bitten by it.)
Tonight in a fight you held back the killing blow — without quite knowing why — on a
figure you would, two days later, find at Gravewatch's inn buying you an ale. A previous
you had killed them. A previous you had regretted it. Your hand has been taught patience.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a kill held back by hands taught by a previous you's regret.)
Tonight you said your pet's name — and realised, after, that the name was one a
previous you had given to a previous pet. The name had stayed in you. The pet, today,
responded to it as if they had always been called this. The kingdom has decided about names.
Tonight you walked a zone you had walked many times — across many runs — and you
walked it in one fewer step than you have ever walked it in before. The shape of the
zone has shrunk for you, by one step. The kingdom rewards the climber who has paid in selves.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a zone walked by you in one fewer step than ever before.)
Tonight you had a conversation with an NPC that a previous you had failed — said the
wrong thing, walked away, never came back. This you, in the same conversation, knew
exactly what to say. They opened to you. They told you the thing the previous you needed to hear.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a conversation a previous you failed, this you completed.)
Today you passed a stranger on the road who, you saw clearly, had been about to speak.
They had opened their mouth. You did not stop. Their mouth closed. You will not, in
this run or any, know what they had to say. The road has it. The road keeps it from you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a sentence you walked past hearing because you did not slow.)
The widow on the second bench in Gravewatch — you have walked past her many times now.
Tonight you did not ask her name. You had decided you would, one of these times. You
did not. You walked on. She watched you go. The bench, behind you, kept her shape.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a widow's name unasked, this run, in any.)
On the road today there was a small figure under a tree, breathing slowly. You did not
stop. You did not know them. You did not have time. You walked on. They went the rest
of the way alone. Some other run, some other you, would have stopped. This you did not.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — a death you walked past while there was still time to sit.)
Today a child in the square spoke to you — a question, you didn't quite catch what.
You smiled vaguely and walked on. The child's face fell. You did not see it fall. You
saw it after, in memory. The child will grow up. You will not see them again. They asked.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a child's question unanswered by you on a day you had time.)
An old one in the square, on the bench by the well, was holding their hand out — not
for alms. To hold. You did not take it. You did not feel up to the holding. You walked.
The hand stayed out a count. Then it withdrew. They will hold someone else's. Not yours.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — an old one's hand offered, by you, refused without speaking.)
Your companion, tonight, was telling you something — slowly, working their way to it.
You cut them off mid-thought. You did not mean to. You did. The sentence they were
building never finished. They will not, in this run, try to say it again. You will not hear it.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a sentence from your companion you did not let arrive.)
Today, in passing, you walked by someone whose face you would, in any other run, have
stopped for. An old friend. Tonight you didn't recognize them. The Pall has taken their
face from you, slowly. They saw you not see them. They walked on. You will not, again.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — an old friend you did not, today, recognize.)
Tonight at the inn the innkeeper did, for you, a small specific kindness. You felt it.
You did not, aloud, thank her. You thanked her in your head. The thanks-in-the-head
did not, in this run, reach her. She will go to bed not knowing. You will go to bed knowing.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a thanks you owed but did not say aloud.)
Today you walked past Atrél's altar without kneeling. You had reason. You always have
reason. The altar did not, in this run, get the kneel from you. Atrél did not, in this
run, get the count of your weight on the flagstone. The altar will, in your life, remember.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a kneel withheld by you from an altar that asks for none.)
You passed a grave today of someone you knew. You knew the name. You did not say the
name aloud. Said names land. Unsaid names drift. The name will drift, this run. You
will say it next time. There will be no next time. You will say it next run, perhaps.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a friend's name unsaid at the place that asks for it.)
At a fork today there was a smaller, less-walked path. You took the bigger. The smaller
path led somewhere — somewhere you would, in some other run, have seen. You did not see.
The smaller path went on without you. It was not, in this run, walked by your foot.
Today on the climb you came around a turn that opened on a view — the kingdom spread
below, the light particular. You did not pause. You had work. The view watched you pass.
You will not, in this run, see that light again from that angle. The angle is in someone else's.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a view you walked through without granting it the count of looking.)
There was a well by the road today — clean, cold, the rope new. You walked past. You
were not thirsty. Some other you, in some other run, would have drunk. Your body, in
this run, will be tireder for the not-drinking. The well will be a well for the next walker.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a well unused by you on a day you walked past it.)
There is a coin-well at the Crossroads — small, the bottom littered with the wishes
of a generation. You did not throw a coin in today. You did not have a wish you trusted.
Many others did. Some of theirs are in the bottom, waiting. None of yours, this run. None.
Gravewatch's open kitchen — the one with the wide door, the cook who sings — had stew
tonight. You did not stop. You ate at the inn. The stew, today, was the kind that would have been remembered.
On the corner near the bell-tower there was a player today — a small one, a stringed
instrument. You did not stop. The song they were playing was not one you knew. It was
going to be one you knew, if you had stopped. The song finished without you in the audience.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a song you did not stop for that would have stayed with you.)
In Mourncross tonight, the third house on the second street had a lit window. You did
not go in. You had been told not to. You did not. There was someone in there. There is.
Today at a branching you considered going back to a zone you had loved. You did not.
The path was longer. The work pressed. You went the other way. The zone you loved is
still there. You will not, in this run, see it again. Other yous have. Not this you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a zone you loved and chose, today, not to return to.)
Gravewatch has, in its third street, a small bath-house — hot stones, the kingdom's old
way. You did not go in today. You meant to. You did not. Your body will be tireder from
now on, in the run, than it would have been if you had taken the bath. You did not.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a bath you did not take that your body needed.)
Today at a stall you saw a thing — small, the kind of thing you would, in any other
run, have bought. You did not. You had been frugal. You walked away. The thing was bought by someone else the next morning.
Today you came to a door that was, you sensed, unlocked. You did not try. You walked
on. The door stayed unlocked, this run. The door will be locked by morning. Whatever
was inside the door for you to find, you did not find. Some other run, you would have.
There was a stair to an upper room you noticed today. You did not climb. The stair was
narrow, the room above quiet. You had reason not to climb. The upper room kept what it
had for you, but you were not, in this run, the one who took it. You walked back down.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a stair not taken by you that was meant to be taken once.)
In the chamber today you came to an inner door that was ajar. You did not push it
further open. You had been told not to. You did not. The door is the same shade of ajar
as you left it. The next visitor will push, perhaps. Not you. Not this you. Not this run.
There was a door today you had been meaning to knock on. You came to it. You did not.
You had reason. You always have. The person inside did not, this run, hear your knock.
They are still in there. They will, in some other run, hear your knock. Not this run.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a door you came to and did not knock on, today.)
There is a shop at the corner of the Crossroads that is rarely open. It was open today.
You did not go in. You meant to. You walked past. The keeper saw you walk past. They did not, you suspect, mind.
Today you opened a door, took what you needed, and went without closing it. You did
not mean to leave it. You did. Whatever lived inside the room will, this night, walk
out into the kingdom. You will not be there to put it back. You walked on. The door is open.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a door you opened and left open that should have been closed.)
Tonight at the saving-place you found, in your pack, a small folded letter. The wax
seal had your old mark. From a previous you. You did not break the seal. You set the
letter aside for later. There is no later. The letter is still sealed. You will not, this run.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a letter from a previous you to this you, left unopened.)
Tonight in the Chronicle room you had — clearly — a particular page in mind to find.
You forgot, when you got there. You read other pages. You left. The page you had been
meaning to find is still there. It will be there when you come back. There is no when.
In a room you stayed in tonight, under the bed, there was a small wooden box. You knew
it was there. You did not open it. You had no claim. You did not. The box is still
under the bed. The box will, you think, contain a thing meant for you. You did not.
In the yard today there was a small grave with your name — a previous you's. You had
been told what was buried with you. You did not unbury. You did not, in this run, take
back the thing that had gone in with you. The grave is intact. The thing is, still, theirs.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a thing you buried as someone else and could have unburied.)
Today you stopped reading two pages before the end. You had had enough. The two pages
would have changed how you read the whole book. You did not read them. The book is
shelved. Some other reader will. You will not, in this run, learn the thing the end said.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a book stopped two pages before the change.)
There was a letter for you at the inn today, left at the front desk by hand. You did
not open it. You meant to before bed. You forgot. The letter is still in your pack.
It will, in this run, stay unread. The hand that wrote it is somewhere doing other things.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a letter left for you that you did not read in time.)
In the page you read today there were margin-notes — small, in a hand you almost knew.
You did not read them. You read the body of the page. The margin was the whole point.
The next reader will. They will be confused. The margin remains for them. You did not.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a margin you did not read that was the page's whole point.)
Tonight someone you knew started a sentence — left it hanging in mid-air, looking at
you to finish it. The right ending was clear. You did not finish. They had to finish
the sentence themselves. They finished it differently than you would have. You missed your turn.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a sentence you did not finish that was left, deliberately, open.)
Today the page you finished reading had a last line that needed to be spoken — said
aloud, into the air, so the room could hear. You read it in your head. The room did
not hear. The line did not, in this run, take. It will take, someone else's run. Not this.
On a hook in a kitchen today there was a recipe — old, smoke-darkened, the writing the
kingdom's old way. You did not read it. You had been hungry, then less hungry. The
recipe stayed on the hook. Someone else will read it. Not you. You will not eat that meal.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a recipe you did not read that would have fed you twice.)
Today a signpost had something on the back. You walked past it without going around.
Whatever was on the back, you did not read. The sign you read on the front said one thing. The back said something else.
Tonight you came across a note in a friend's handwriting — left somewhere, for you, on
the kingdom's slow message-net. You folded it. You meant to read it at the inn. You did
not. The note is still in your coat. You will not, in this run, take it back out.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a friend's handwriting you did not, in time, read.)
Today you read part of a grave-marker — the name. You did not read the rest. The rest
was the dates, the small line about who they had been. You walked on. The grave is in
the kingdom. The dates are in the stone. You did not know them. You will not, this run.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a grave-marker you read only the name of.)
On a page today there was a drawing. The drawing was the message. You did not read the
drawing — you read the text below and walked on. The drawing was, you understand now,
what the page was for. You did not see what the drawing was. The page kept it. From you.
Tonight a grief you had been carrying — small, manageable, the kind that does not, on
its own, hurt — came up to you and asked you to sit with it. You did not. You said you
were busy. The grief sat down alone. The grief will, in this run, not get sat with again.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a grief you did not sit with on a night you had time.)
Tonight at the saving-place, alone, you came up to a count where you could have cried.
You did not. You held it. You went to sleep dry-eyed. The crying went into your shoulders.
Your shoulders will be tireder for it. You will not, in this run, cry that crying. It stays.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a crying held back at a count where it could have come.)
Tonight at the saving-place you had paper, ink, a candle, and a person you meant to
write a letter to. You did not write. You meant to. You closed the ink. The paper is
still folded in your pack. The person will not, in this run, get the letter.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a letter you meant to write tonight and did not.)
Today you passed someone you owed an apology to. You did not, today, give it. You had
decided you would. You did not. The owed apology stays owed, this run. They will go
to sleep tonight knowing you did not. So will you. The owing will be heavier from now.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — an apology owed by you and not, in this run, paid.)
Tonight your companion, seeing you tired, opened their arms — the simple gesture, the
offer of holding. You did not step into it. You patted their shoulder. You went to bed.
Some other you would have stepped in. The companion did not, in this run, hold you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a holding offered you that you did not step into.)
Tonight you had the option of an hour off — to sit somewhere, to do nothing, to let
your body lay flat. You did not take it. You walked the next zone. Your body kept
score. The score will not be paid in this run. The hour is the kingdom's now, not yours.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — an hour off your body needed that you did not take.)
Today your companion offered, without asking, to carry one of your griefs for the hour
of the walking. You said no. You did not, you said, want them to carry it. They had
wanted to. They put their hand back at their side. The grief is still yours. They went quieter.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a companion's offer to carry a grief that you refused.)
Tonight you did a thing you were proud of. You did not tell anyone. You went to bed
with the pride wholly inside you. Someone else has not heard. The pride did not, in this run, become a thing shared.
Tonight at the saving-place there was a paper in your pack — old, of yours, the kind
you have been meaning to burn. You did not burn it. You held it over the candle. You
put it back. The paper will be in your pack still, in your next save. You will not.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a paper you should have burned and chose, tonight, to keep.)
Tonight at the saving-place, alone, you had the chance to say *thank you* — quietly,
to the kingdom — for the day's small mercies. You did not. You went to bed. The
kingdom did not, in this run, hear from you the thanks. The kingdom keeps these accounts.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a thanks you owed the kingdom you walked through.)
Tonight, after a fight, the poison had been in you long enough that you had learned —
as you fought, breath by breath — to breathe slowly through the burn of it. The lesson
stayed after the poison cleared. You will breathe slower in the bad beats now. It helps.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a slow-breath lesson the poison taught you to keep.)
After a poison wound today the bed of your thumbnail — the left, the one you favour —
stayed faintly bluish. Not painful. Coloured. The kingdom's poisons leave such tints.
You will look at the thumb sometimes and remember what put it there. The tint will not fade.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a poison-tint in your thumbnail that you will see every day.)
Tonight, mid-fight, an old voice came up to you — somebody you used to know, who used
to gather. They told you which leaf to chew when the poison takes. You chewed. The
poison cleared faster. The voice has done you a kindness. You will remember the leaf.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a leaf-name a long-gone friend told you between strikes.)
Tonight at the saving-place, in the long sleep after a poisoned day, you did not dream.
Not for any of the night. The poison had taken your dreams. The dreams are, you suspect,
in the poison's account now. You will dream less from now on. The kingdom kept them.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a night of dreams the poison kept and did not give back.)
Mid-fight tonight the poison in your blood, on its third tick, briefly tightened your
vision and showed you the enemy's next strike before they made it. The poison was, in
its small way, helping. You blocked. The poison subsided. You will know the trick now.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a moment of unwelcome clarity the poison gave you, mid-tick.)
After the poison cleared tonight you swore off — to yourself, alone — a food you used
to love. The food had nothing to do with the poison. Your body decided. You will not eat the food again, this run.
On the empty bottle of antivenom you used tonight there was a small label, hand-written.
An apprentice's hand. Wobbly letters. They had been new to the labelling. They had
saved your life. You will, in some kindly impulse, write back. You don't know where they are.
Tonight the poison reached your hand and stopped — at the third knuckle of the index
finger, the dark line as visible as a marker. The line stopped there. The poison did
not take the finger. You will, every time you make a fist, see the line. It will fade slowly.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a finger the poison reached and did not, in the end, take.)
Today, after a poisoned day, you found yourself unable to sing the song you usually
hum on the walk. The notes would not come. The poison had taken them, for the day. They
did not, in the end, come back. The song is in the kingdom's hands. Not yours. Not today.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a song the poison kept that did not, today, come back.)
Tonight in Gravewatch a healer — one of the old ones, the kind that the kingdom keeps
in alleyways — pressed their dry lips to your forehead after working the last of the
poison out of you. *Walk steady,* they said. You walked. You felt the steadiness held.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a healer's dry kiss to your forehead, the kingdom's old way.)
After a burning fight tonight, the inside of your collar smelled of smoke for hours.
You washed. The smell did not, fully, come out. The next time you sweat, it will come
back. From now on, in heat, you will smell of one of those fights. You can live with it.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — smoke in your collar that the wash did not, fully, take out.)
Tonight, mid-burning, your body — without your asking — dropped you to the ground and
rolled you. The kingdom's old way of putting out a fire on a person. You did not know
you knew. The roll worked. The flame went. You will, from now on, know the trick.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the drop-and-roll your body knew in the bad beat without asking.)
Mid-fight tonight a stranger — somebody who had been watching — threw their cloak over
you to smother a flame. You were knocked, briefly, into the dark of the wool. They
stamped. The flame went. You did not, you realise, get the stranger's name. The cloak is yours now.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a cloak a stranger threw to put you out, kept by you after.)
On your forearm, where the burn settled, the scar — by the time it healed — was the
shape of a word. The kingdom's old script, the rite-tongue. The word means *kept.* You
did not, this run, choose the word. The body chose it. You will look at it sometimes.
Tonight the burn went deeper than it should have. Underneath, a thing you had not
thought of in years — an old anger, the small kind that festers — kindled. You will
carry the anger forward. It will burn quietly. You can use it. It will use you a little.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — an old anger the fight kindled that will not, now, fully bank.)
After burning tonight you have learned, with some certainty, to offer water — to others —
before yourself. The body knows. A burned person nearby is, you understand, a priority
over a thirsty self. You will, from now on, give the cup before you take it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an offer-first manner the burning taught you to keep.)
Tonight, lighting a candle at the saving-place, the match shook in your hand and the
flame would not come. You waited the body out. The body, since the bad burn, does not
make light easily. You will, from now on, take an extra count to start a fire.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a body that hesitates around small flames since the burning.)
Tonight you took a small cinder — black, the size of a thumbnail — from the spent fire
of an enemy. You put it in your pocket. It will not, now, leave the pocket. It is yours.
