It is a small reminder. Your next swing will be — by a count — angrier. The cinder agrees.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a cinder from a fire that tried to take you, kept in your pocket.)
Tonight the smith, seeing the burn on your forearm, named the kind of fire that made
it. *That's a censer-shade's fire,* she said. She had been burned by one too. She
showed you her own scar. You both stood quiet a count. The Choir is not finished with either of you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a burn the smith named, and an old scar shown back in return.)
In the ash of tonight's burning fight there was a small unburned object — a wooden
carving the size of a thumb, the kind a child whittles. The fire had stepped around it.
You took it. It is in your pocket. The fire chose, somewhere in itself, to be kind. So can you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a kindness the fire kept for you to find after.)
Tonight in the fight a drop of your own blood — from a high wound, near your hairline —
ran down your face and dried on your chin. You did not wipe it. You walked the next zone
with it there. People you passed saw it. They did not comment. They had seen blood before.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a drop of your own blood you walked a zone with, deliberately not wiped.)
Mid-fight tonight, with the bleeding bad, you pressed a cloth — torn from your own
sleeve — to the wound, hard, the kingdom's way. The bleeding slowed. The fight finished.
Your sleeve is short of cloth now. Your body, from now on, will know to press first.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a cloth-pressing the body learned in the worst beat of the bleed.)
Tonight, coming back from a bleeding day, you steadied yourself on a doorframe in
Gravewatch. Your palm left a print. The keeper of the house came out, saw the print,
and did not, in the end, wipe it away. The print is on the doorframe. It will remain.
A dog — small, lean, scarred — followed you back to Gravewatch tonight from where the
bleeding had been. They had smelled the blood. They walked at a distance. They sat at
your inn door. They were gone in the morning. They had, for one night, decided about you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a dog who decided to walk you home through the dusk of a bleeding day.)
Tonight a widow in the square looked at the way you held your side — the angle of the
favouring — and said quietly, *I had a husband who bled in that exact way.* She did not
say what happened to him. You did not ask. She gave you a small dry biscuit. You took it.
In the worst of the bleeding tonight you said — under your breath, almost without
thinking — the name of someone you loved. The blood, at the saying, slowed. The kingdom
has small mercies. The name was a key. You will say it again if the bleeding starts again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a name that slowed a bleeding when you said it aloud.)
Tonight in the Chronicle, turning a page with bloodied hands you had not, fully,
washed, you left a small print at the margin. The clerk saw. The clerk did not, in
the end, scrub it. *The page has a record now,* she said. *Of you reading.* She turned a page for you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Chronicle margin marked, by your hand, in your own blood.)
Tonight you found, washing, that some of the blood on your tunic was not yours. From a
fight you don't remember being touched by another's wound. Whoever had been with you,
bleeding, had bled onto your clothes. You laundered. The stain has set in. They are with you.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — somebody else's bleeding kept on your tunic until it set.)
Tonight, after a bleeding fight, you came back into Gravewatch and an old one — at
the gate, sitting — wordlessly handed you a folded clean cloth. They had been waiting
with the cloth for someone who needed it. You took it. You used it. The cloth is yours now.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a clean cloth an old one had been keeping for the next bleeder.)
Tonight at the saving-place you dreamt the bleed again. Not the fight. The bleeding.
Slow. The way your body decided to keep going. You woke quietly. The dream was not a
nightmare. It was instruction. The body was teaching the body. You can, in waking, also keep going.
Tonight you were stunned, briefly, in the bad beat. The count of seconds you lost is
not, properly, in your memory. Your day is shorter by that count. The count is the
kingdom's. You will not, in this run, get it back. You can carry the small absence.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a count of seconds the stun took out of your day.)
Coming back from the stun tonight, the room — the fight, the noise — was, for one
long count, quieter than it should have been. As if the world had been holding still
with you. Then sound returned. You finished. Some part of you stayed in the quieter room.
In the stun tonight you dropped a small thing from your pouch — you don't know what.
You looked, after. The pouch is shorter by one thing. The thing is on the ground of
the fight-zone. The thing will not be picked up by you. You walked on. The thing stays.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — something small dropped from your pouch during the stun and left.)
In the stun tonight, when your knees gave, somebody — your companion, your hireling,
the pet, you don't remember which — caught your shoulder. You did not, in this fight,
go down. The hand that caught you was warm. You will not forget it was caught.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a hand on your shoulder in a stun that did not let you fall.)
After tonight's stun, a metallic taste settled in your mouth and has not, by morning,
left. Your tongue tells you it is there. The kingdom's stuns leave it sometimes. You
will eat with a faint underlay of metal from now on. Eating will be a little slower.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a metallic taste a stun left that will not, in this run, fade.)
Coming back from the stun tonight you, before your full self had returned, said a name
aloud — somebody you had not thought of in a long time. Your old self said it. You
asked your own mouth, after, why. Your mouth would not, in this run, tell you why.
After being stunned tonight, your body — the next swing — braced before the strike
landed. Knees soft, shoulder turned in. The brace was correct. You learned it from being
knocked out of your stance the last count. The body teaches itself. You will brace from now on.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a brace-stance the body learned from a stun, the once.)
In the stun tonight your companion took your sleeve and held it tight. They did not
let go until you were back. You did not know they had. You found out from the cuff of
the sleeve, after — slightly stretched. They are not letting you go easily from now on.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a companion holding your sleeve through a stun until you came back.)
After the stun cleared tonight you found you had been humming — out of habit, while
you came back — a tune you didn't know you knew. The tune was old. The tune was good.
It is in your throat now. You will hum it on the road. It will keep your step steady.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a tune the stun left in your throat that you will use to walk.)
After the stun tonight, an NPC at your side — whoever happened to be there — said,
very quietly, *welcome back.* They did not make a thing of it. You did not either. The
welcome was the small kingdom-old kind. You stood up under it. You felt the welcoming.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a *welcome back* given to you after a stun by someone next to you.)
Tonight a potion you drank — to cure a wound — tasted faintly of one your grandmother
used to brew when you were small. The kingdom's potions, you understand, are old recipes
carried forward. Your grandmother's was, somewhere, the same. You stand straighter knowing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a potion whose taste matched one your grandmother once brewed.)
Tonight, with the brace on, a strike that should have landed glanced off your shoulder.
You did not feel it land. You felt the deflection. The shoulder knows now what bracing
feels like, properly. It will brace itself before you tell it to, from now on.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a brace-glance the shoulder learned on its own to do.)
Tonight with the evasion in your blood, your feet stepped aside — without your asking —
from a strike. The step was small. The step worked. Your feet have, from now on, a
habit of small lateral steps. You will, when nervous, step sideways without thinking.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a step-aside the body learned that will travel with you.)
Tonight, walking, you recognized — without naming — a plant by the path that you could,
if you ate it, use to cure a poison. The body learned it from a cured poison. Hunters
know the plant. You did not know you had become a hunter. You have, in a small way, become one.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a wild plant the body learned to recognize from a curing.)
After a cleansing tonight — a status lifted, the body settled — your breath was, for
the next hour, quieter than it normally is. Not weaker. Cleaner. The kingdom does not
often clean a body. Tonight it did. You will, from now on, breathe a little cleaner.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a quietness in the breath the cleansing left in you.)
Tonight your brace held against the third strike — the one that, in fights past, has
broken your guard. The brace held. The strike turned. You did not, in this fight, go
down. The brace will, from now on, hold against the third. The body has crossed a count.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a brace that held a strike that used to break your guard.)
Tonight a curing consumable you ate — a piece of bread, a small fruit — tasted of a
kitchen long gone. Not your grandmother's. Somebody's. The kingdom keeps its old
kitchens in its provisions. You ate the kitchen. It went with you. You did not say.
Tonight, evading, you tilted your head — just a finger's width — and a strike that
would have caught your temple missed by exactly that finger. The tilt was learned. Your
neck knows now. From now on, in any threat, your head will tilt the right way for you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a head-tilt the neck learned that has saved you a temple-strike.)
Tonight, drinking a curing potion, you knelt — without thinking — the kingdom's old way
of taking medicine. You did not know you knew. The body knew. The body has, somewhere
in it, the manners of an older Mournhold. You stood up under them. You will kneel again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a kneeling-to-drink the body kept from older Mournhold.)
Tonight, after clearing the last of a particularly nasty status — burn, poison, both —
you stood and the kingdom, faintly, thanked you. Not Atrél. The kingdom itself. The
thanks landed softly. You don't get thanked often by Mournhold. You did, tonight. You stood taller.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a thanks from the kingdom for clearing a status it had decided was bad.)
Tonight, walking, you found that you had carried — without effort — a long silence
across an hour of the path. Not a heavy silence. A keeper's. The silence felt familiar
and old. The previous Warden's silence. You are walking into the work. You do not mind.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a keeper's silence the body has begun to carry on its own.)
Tonight at the saving you understood — without anyone telling you — that the Warden
role is waiting for you. Specifically you. Not just any climber. The kingdom has been
preparing the keeping for the shape of you. You said yes, in your head. The shape settled.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — an understanding accepted of the role waiting at the Summit.)
Tonight on the road the Pall walked three steps with you. Not behind. Beside. Then —
without ceremony — it withdrew. Three steps. The count is important in the kingdom's
old way. It was, you understand, the formal escort of one keeper-to-be by the one in office.
Tonight at the altar Atrél did not, as Atrél sometimes does, offer you another path.
Atrél has, tonight, decided not to argue. Atrél stood beside you in silence and let
you make the choice you are making. The choice is the Warden's. Atrél is letting you go.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — Atrél's silence at the altar tonight, accepted the Warden choice.)
Tonight on the climb you understood the Summit's ground — the strange softness — is
the ground a keeper walks. Not rock. The ground of the position. Your boot will, when
you get there, sink slightly. As the previous Warden's boot sank. As theirs did.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a recognition of the Summit's ground as keeper's ground.)
Tonight in the Chronicle the clerk turned to your page and — already — had marked it
*Warden, presumed.* You saw the ink. She did not look up. The kingdom is preparing for
the keeping. You did not, in the end, ask her to change the line. You let her keep it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Chronicle entry that anticipates your becoming the Warden.)
Tonight on the climb your breath, in the thinning air, steamed easier than it should
have. The lungs are adjusting. The Pall is making your lungs ready. By the time you
reach the top, the lungs of the Warden will be your lungs. The transition has begun.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — lungs adjusting to the Pall's air ahead of arrival.)
Tonight, humming on the path, you started a song you had not heard before. It came up
from inside. It is not a song; it is a count. The Pall's count — the rhythm a keeper
keeps. The count steadied your stride. The song will be yours from the Summit on.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a keeper's count that the body began to know without lesson.)
Tonight at the Crossroads the lampkeeper, lighting the evening row, called you *Warden*
— quietly, almost without thinking, the way one calls a thing by its right name. She
did not correct herself. She had decided. You did not, in the end, correct her either.
Tonight at Gravewatch's inn, leaving for the climb, you stood up from your chair and —
as is the kingdom's old way for a climber who will not come back the usual way — you
left it pushed back, not tucked in. The next climber will sit there. They will see the chair waiting.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a chair left in the old way for the next climber to find.)
Tonight at Atrél's altar you set a grief down — one of the big ones — and it set down
easily. You had not, before, been able to. The altar took it without comment. Atrél
looked at you in something like satisfaction. The setting-down is the practice. You are practicing.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a grief set down easily that, before, you could not put down.)
Tonight the silence you held with Atrél at the altar lasted longer than any before. A
whole count of the candle. Neither of you spoke. Neither of you needed to. The silence
was the conversation. You will, from now on, hold longer silences with the kingdom.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a longer silence with Atrél than you have, before, sustained.)
Tonight Atrél began a sentence. They paused. You finished it for them — correctly, in
the old tongue, in the form a priest would use. Atrél smiled — the small smile they
rarely show. You both stood quiet a count. You are coming to share a vocabulary.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a sentence of Atrél's finished correctly by you in the old tongue.)
Tonight one of the Hollowed you had laid to rest — earlier in the run, a small unceremonious
rite — appeared briefly at the edge of your camp. Not threatening. Thanking. Soundlessly.
Then it was gone. You have begun, the kingdom is saying, the work that will be the peace.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a laid-to-rest Hollowed appearing once to thank you, soundlessly.)
Tonight, walking, you noticed the kingdom was — by a count — quieter as you passed. The
Hollowed-step that usually rustles along behind, the small Pall-hum at the road's edge —
they were quieter for you. Atrél's peace, even before the ending, has begun to settle.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a quieter kingdom-edge under your step on the peace-approach.)
Tonight at the shrine Atrél — without comment — laid a small old robe across your
shoulders. A priest's. You wore it for the night. It was warmer than your coat. You
took it off before leaving. Atrél did not, in the end, ask you to leave it behind.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a priest's robe across your shoulders at the shrine for a night.)
Tonight you spoke to a Hollowed in passing — a sentence, only, *go well* in the old
tongue — and the Hollowed inclined their head and went. The peace-tongue is becoming
yours. You did not know the syllables a month ago. The kingdom is teaching them as you go.
(+1 Attack, permanently — peace-tongue syllables that the kingdom is teaching you in passing.)
Tonight at the saving-place you, without your companion seeing, set a small grief of
theirs down at Atrél's altar — one you had been carrying for them. Atrél took it. Your
companion will not, in this run, notice the lightening. The lightening is theirs anyway.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a grief of your companion's that you, secretly, laid at Atrél's altar.)
Tonight a count settled in your head — the number Tálva had given you, of the silenced
and the starved. The number is in you. It is not, anymore, a fact. It is a weight.
You can carry the weight. You will carry it to wherever Tálva's path takes you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a count of the dead settled inside you as weight, not fact.)
Tonight at the saving-place there was, in your pack, a small folded paper you did not
put there. Inside: a single name. In Tálva's writing. They had walked, at some hour,
to your camp and left it. You said the name aloud once. The kingdom heard it added.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a name Tálva walked, in the night, to your camp to give you.)
Tonight the Hidden Hold — the deep one, the one Tálva keeps — felt, walking through it,
yours. As if you had lived here. As if you had been one of the starved. The hold accepted
you. You did not feel the cold the way you used to. You will breathe its air easier.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — the Hidden Hold accepting your breath as native.)
Tonight one of Tálva's descendants — the youngest, the quiet one — called you *kin* in
passing. Not friend. Kin. The kingdom uses the word carefully. The descendant was using
it carefully. You did not, in the end, correct them. The naming, once given, stays given.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a descendant of Tálva's calling you kin, deliberately.)
Tonight you have begun to carry a small bread in your pack — kept aside, not eaten —
in case you meet someone the Reckoning needs you to feed. The bread is yours to give.
You will know who when you see them. The kingdom will let you know. Tálva will let you know.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a bread kept aside in your pack for the Reckoning's giving.)
Tonight at the Crossroads, on the back of the signpost, you carved — small, with the
edge of your knife — one of the names Tálva had given you. It is now in the wood. The
next traveller will see it. The next traveller may not know who. The name is added.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Reckoning-name carved by you into the back of the Crossroads signpost.)
Tonight you understood — the way these things land — that the kingdom owes you a
silence. The silence the silenced were never given. You will, when you arrive at the Reckoning, ask for it.
Tonight in the hold a hand — the kind that no longer carries weight, that has not for
a long count — pressed against yours, briefly, through stone. Then went. You did not
see whose. You felt the thinness of the fingers. The kingdom's old starved are with you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a starved hand pressed through stone briefly against yours.)
Tonight in the Bone Tomb, three stones warmed at once when you knelt — not one, the
way it has been. The kingdom's old dead are agreeing about you. Cael, through the wall,
did not need to say. You will be brought to the seal. The seal is keeping its date.
(+1 Defense, permanently — three stones warming at once, a kingdom-old assent.)
Tonight in the Tomb a dead priest — one of the niches that, you had noticed, was the
kind kept for the kingdom's old priests — said your name, soundlessly, through the wall.
You said yes. The priest withdrew. The kingdom's old priesthood now knows your name.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — your name said by a dead priest into the stone of the Bone Tomb.)
Tonight you put your palm to the third wall — the one with Cael's old seal — and the
stone, under your hand, eased. Not the seal pressing. The wall yielding. As if the
wall had been waiting for the right hand. Your hand. The wall agreed about you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the third wall easing under your palm as if it had been waiting.)
Tonight Cael, through the stone, taught you the three words. The Old Seal's words.
You will not say them aloud yet. You will, when you come back the final time. The
words sit in your throat now. Your throat will hold them safely. Cael trusts your throat.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the three words of the Old Seal, kept in your throat by Cael's teaching.)
Tonight the bone dust in your hair, after the Tomb, will not — for any combing — leave.
You will, the rest of this run, walk with a faint pale dust in your hair. The kingdom
marks the next sealer this way. You are visible to those who know what to look for.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — bone dust the kingdom marked you with that will not, this run, leave.)
Tonight at the Tomb a small stone from the priest-row, the size of a thumbnail, came
loose into your hand. Cael, through the wall, said *keep it.* You did. It rides in
your pocket. It will be needed at the sealing. You don't know how. Cael does.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a priest-stone given to you by Cael against the day of the sealing.)
Tonight, speaking through the wall to Cael, the trick — the count of the breath, the
lay of the palm — came easily. You did not have to remember. The body had become a
speaker-through-stone. You can do this with any wall now. You will when you need to.
(+1 Defense, permanently — speaking-through-stone become natural to the body.)
Tonight the air in the Tomb — for one count — smelled of a kitchen. Onions, broth.
The kitchen of a priest's house, long demolished. The dead remember kitchens. Cael
did not comment. You did not ask. You walked the row in the kitchen-smell.
Tonight, walking, you walked into a zone you have, before, cleansed. The zone felt —
for the first time — yours. Not the kingdom's, not the Pall's. The zone you have
lifted. You will lift more. The kingdom is being given back, by you, to itself.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a cleansed zone that felt, today, like your own ground.)
Tonight, breathing, the air of one of your cleansed zones — for one count — tasted
sweet. The kingdom does not, properly, do sweet. You have given it back the capacity.
Your lungs will, from now on, be steadier in the sweetness. The capacity will spread.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — sweetness in the air of a zone you, before, cleansed.)
Tonight, arriving, a child in the cleansed zone was singing. Children do not, in
Mournhold, much sing anymore. The Pall took the songs. This child has them back. Some
of the songs are coming back. They are coming back into the children first. Your work is showing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a child singing in a cleansed zone, the kingdom's old songs returning.)
Tonight a widow in Gravewatch said *thank you, for the year.* You did not understand,
at first. She meant the cleansing you had done — three runs ago. The widow had been
counting. The widow had been thanking you across the runs. Tonight you, finally, heard it.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a thanks across runs given to you for a cleansing you barely remember doing.)
Tonight in the Reach — long dead — there was, for one count, a smell of grass. The
field, briefly, remembered being a field. The smell went again. The remembering took.
You did, in some other run, a thing here. The field has not, fully, forgotten.
Tonight in the Chronicle the clerk — who has known many of you — looked up and her
face was, today, softer. As if she had decided you, this one, might do the purifying.
She did not say. She turned to your page. Something in the page had changed. She nodded.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Chronicle clerk's softening when she saw, this run, you.)
Tonight you have begun to hum a song that you, the previous time, could not hum. The
Pall had taken pieces of it. Tonight a piece has come back. Not all. One verse. The
verse is yours again. The Pall is giving back, slowly, what it has been keeping.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a verse of a song the Pall had been keeping, returned to your humming.)
Tonight in the Witherwood the brush did not, in any of its rustlings, treat you as a
stranger. The wood has decided about you. You are not, anymore, a threat. You are the
one who has cleared this air before. You can move through the forest as a known thing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Witherwood recognizing you as a known walker on the purify-path.)
Tonight at the saving-place there was — chalked, you don't know by what — a small glyph
on the wall by your bed. You recognized it. The Forgotten Thing's mark. Left for you
by whatever it had become. You did not, in the end, scrub it. The glyph will travel with you.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a Forgotten Thing's glyph chalked by your bed for you to keep.)
Tonight the word — the one the Forgotten Thing had given you at the end of its fight —
spoke up in your mouth. It wanted out. You did not, today, say it. You will. The word
is becoming impatient. The kingdom waits to hear it. So do you. You stand straighter.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the Forgotten Thing's gifted word growing more impatient in your mouth.)
Tonight the Pall — usually, lately, your near-companion — did not walk with you. It
kept a distance. The Pall has, you understand, decided to be careful around you. You
are carrying a truth it does not, yet, want to disturb. You walked on, alone of it.
Tonight a book in the Library — one of the half-burned ones — opened in your hand to a
page that had not, before, been there. The page had words on it. The words were the
Truth you have been carrying. Now written. Now real. You closed the book. You kept the page.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a page of the hidden Truth that wrote itself in your hand.)
Tonight Atrél — who answers, who does not, properly, ask — asked you a question. They
wanted to know what the Forgotten Thing had given you. You did not, tonight, tell. Atrél
did not press. They have become curious. The kingdom is becoming curious about you.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — Atrél asking you, for once, a question instead of answering one.)
Tonight in the Choir's empty stalls, where the priests used to stand, a silhouette
— old, of one of the old priests, the kind that had been at the rite — turned to look
at you. Recognized you. Whatever it saw, it filed away. It turned back. You walked through.
Tonight at the saving you said — quietly, to no one, only to the kingdom — a sentence.
You felt the kingdom listen. The whole of it. It is not, you understand, a thing of
the past anymore. The kingdom now hears you. You did not, in the end, say much. You will.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a kingdom that, tonight, listened to a sentence of yours.)
Tonight on the Summit you felt that the place is no longer expecting the Warden's
successor. The place is expecting somebody else. You. Carrying the Truth. The Summit
has had to reset its preparations. The Pall is, for the first time, uncertain. So are you. Good.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a Summit that has had, in your hands, to reset its preparations.)
Tonight at the saving you dreamt of a long corridor. Empty. Lit by no source. The walls
had faces in them you almost knew. You walked the corridor in the dream. You woke at
the door at the end without opening it. The door will be there next time. So will you.
Tonight at the saving you dreamt your mother at a window. She was looking out, not at
you. The window opened to a garden that did not exist. She did not turn. You watched
her watching. The dream was a kindness. You woke held. You stood up easier.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a dream of your mother at a window the kingdom let you have.)
Tonight at the saving you dreamt of a child you might, in a kinder world, have had.
You did not see the face clearly. You saw the small hand. The hand reached for yours.
You woke before it touched. The hand kept reaching, in the dream, after you had left.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a child unborn whose hand reached for yours in the saving-dream.)
Tonight at the saving you slept and did not dream. Not once, not at all. The Pall took
the dreaming. The Pall does this, sometimes, when it is busy. You woke with the count
of the night unaccounted for. The night is in the Pall's books now. Not yours.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a night of dreams the Pall took for itself.)
Tonight the saving — the act of laying yourself out for the disk, palm flat — felt
ceremonial. Not perfunctory. The kingdom's old laying-down. You took longer than usual.
The disk took the save slowly, in respect. You will, from now on, save in this manner.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a saving-ritual the body has come to do with proper care.)
Tonight at waking, after a save, you had a name in your mouth — a name from somewhere,
not yours, not anyone's you've met this run. You said it once aloud. The name took. The
kingdom kept it. You will, from now on, know the name. You will not know what to do with it.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a name in your mouth at waking that the kingdom decided to leave you.)
Tonight at the saving-place the lamp on the wall — that you had not yet lit — was
lit. Faintly. It had decided you were coming. It had decided to be ready. You did not,
in the end, blow it out. You let it burn through the saving. You slept in its light.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a lamp that had decided you were coming and lit itself.)
Tonight you dreamt the previous Warden walking — slow, head down, the kingdom around
them. They turned, in the dream, and saw you watching. They did not, in the dream,
speak. They lifted a hand. You woke. The hand stayed in the air after you were awake.
Tonight at the saving-place you noticed the dust under the bed — undisturbed for many
nights — had, in one corner, the shape of a small footprint. Not yours. Smaller. The
kingdom has, in this room, a small companion. You did not, in the end, sweep the dust.
Tonight you dreamt of Atrél — the face from before the rite, the older one, the one
the kingdom decided to forget. You saw it clearly. You woke with the face slipping
from you, the way old dreams slip. Some of the face stayed. You will, you think, hold it.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — Atrél's first face, briefly held in a saving-dream.)
Tonight at the saving-place you set, without thinking, a small bowl of water by the
head of the bed. The kingdom's old way. For the dead who, in the night, might come
thirsty. You did not need to. You did. The bowl was empty in the morning. They came.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a bowl set out for the dead at the saving-place, drained by morning.)
Tonight at the saving you dreamt the whole route back — Witherwood, Sodden Reach, the
Gullet, all the way. In one count. You walked it in the dream in the time it takes to
breathe. The body knows the way now. The body will know the way in the next walking.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a saving-dream of walking the whole way back in one breath.)
Tonight at the saving you dreamt a house you used to live in. Long ago. Before the
kingdom. The house was not the way you remembered it. The Pall had been in it.
Rearranged the rooms. You woke unsettled. The Pall had been redecorating your old places.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a house from before the kingdom the Pall has redecorated in your sleep.)
Tonight at the saving-place your companion took first watch. While they sat with their
back to the door you slept deeper than you have in nights. Your companion will not, in
the morning, comment. You will both know. You will offer to take first watch tomorrow.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a deeper sleep allowed by a companion's first watch.)
Tonight you dreamt a child naming a grave — the one in Gravewatch's third row, the
unnamed one. The child said the name aloud. You woke with the name in your head. The
child will not, in waking, be there. The name will. You will say it tomorrow at the grave.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a name given to you, in a dream, for an unnamed grave by a child.)
Tonight you dreamt of the kingdom — the whole of it, every zone, every Pall-corner —
breathing. Slow. Long inhales, long exhales. You breathed with it. You woke breathing
to its rhythm. From now on, between zones, you will breathe to the kingdom's rhythm.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a saving-dream of the kingdom breathing that your lungs caught.)
Tonight at the saving-place an owl was on the sill of the window. The owl did not
hunt. Did not move. The owl watched you save. The kingdom uses owls, sometimes, the
way it uses widows — as quiet witnesses. The owl noted the saving. You stood up watched.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an owl on the saving-window keeping the kingdom's notes.)
Tonight at waking you held a word in your mouth that, when you tried to say it, would
not come out. The word was not lost. The word was waiting. The word is something the
kingdom does not, yet, want said. You will, when the right moment comes, say it.
Tonight at the saving-place, in Gravewatch, the chalk marks on the doorpost — the
small protective signs the innkeepers leave — glowed, very faintly, for one count when
you crossed. The marks recognized you. The marks let you pass. You went in. You slept.
Tonight you dreamt a priest — old, neither Atrél nor Vael, somebody else — robing you.
Carefully. The way they robe a priest the kingdom intends to send out. You did not
protest in the dream. You stood for it. The priest, before fading, blessed your hands.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a robing in a dream that the body remembers like a benediction.)
Tonight at the saving-place you scratched, with your knife on the underside of the
saving-table, a single name. Yours. The next person to lift the table will see it.
You are now in the table. The table will, you think, remember the next time you save.
(+1 Defense, permanently — your name scratched into the underside of a saving-table.)
Tonight you dreamt of finishing the climb well. Not the way the climb actually ends.
A version of the ending where everyone you wanted alive was alive. You stayed in the
dream longer than you should have. You woke knowing the dream was the kingdom's gift.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a dream of finishing well the kingdom let you have, for once.)
Tonight at Gravewatch's inn the innkeeper had put an extra pillow — the soft one, the
one she keeps back for particular guests — on your bed. You slept with two pillows. You
have not slept with two in a long count. She did not, in the morning, say. You said thanks.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — an extra pillow the innkeeper kept back for whichever guest you turned out to be.)
Tonight at the inn the room had been recently swept — for you. The floor was clean.
The corners were clean. There were marks of the broom on the floor in the doorway. The
innkeeper had wanted you to come into a clean room. The kingdom is being kind tonight.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a room swept for you with care that you noticed and kept.)
Tonight at the saving, before you slept, you thanked the disk — aloud — for keeping
what it has been keeping. The disk did not respond. The disk does not. The thanks landed.
The disk will, from now on, take your saves a count more carefully. You will know it does.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a thanks said aloud to the disk that has been keeping you.)
Tonight, halfway through a page, the words on it re-arranged themselves under your eye.
Not many — three or four. The new arrangement said a different sentence. You read both.
The page settled. The second sentence is in your head now. The first is in someone else's.
Tonight, turning a page, you noticed the ink — somewhere mid-page — was still drying.
Not old. Today. Somebody had been writing this. Recently. You did not see who. They
left in a hurry. The page is yours now. You will, in your own ink, finish it.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a page whose ink was still drying when you found it.)
Tonight on a page you read, one word — a small one, in the middle of a sentence — had
been carefully underlined. By a hand you don't know. The hand had wanted the next reader
to see this word particularly. You were the next reader. The word is, now, particularly yours.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a word underlined for the next reader who turned out to be you.)
Tonight in the margin of a page there was, scribbled, a small face. A clerk's idle
doodle, perhaps. The face was yours. You don't remember the clerk. The clerk drew
you. The drawing was very careful. The clerk loved you, in some run, in some way.
Tonight the pages of the book were warm under your fingertips. Not just the page you
were reading. The whole book. Body-warm. The book had been held, recently and long, by
somebody who cared about the book. The warmth lingered. You read in the warmth. Easier.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a book whose pages stayed warm under your reading hand.)
Tonight from the book a small pressed flower — colourless from age — fell out. A reader
before you, somewhere, in some other run, had put it there. You picked it up. You put
it back. The flower stays in the book. You will, in some other run, find it again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a pressed flower kept in a discovery-book you have left there for the next reader.)
Tonight you re-read a line and the text — for one count — corrected itself. The wrong
word slid sideways and the right word came in. You saw the change. The kingdom has
decided this line should be true. You read the corrected version. You will not see the wrong one again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a line corrected, under your eye, by the kingdom's invisible editor.)
Tonight you recognized the handwriting in the book. A friend's. A friend who is dead
now. They had written this. You did not know they had. You did not know they wrote at
all. You read the whole thing. You sat the count out. You stand straighter for it.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a book in your dead friend's hand that you, tonight, read.)
Tonight a word on the page — the kingdom's old word for *patient* — made you, without
thinking, say aloud the name of someone you knew. The name took. The kingdom has decided the name and the word belong together.
Tonight, tucked between pages, there was a small threadbare ribbon. Red once. Pink now.
A child's ribbon. You took it. You did not put it back. The ribbon now travels with you.
It rides in your coat. The page that had been marked by it will be re-marked, by you, with a clean one.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a child's threadbare ribbon taken from a discovery-page to ride with you.)
Tonight a whole paragraph of the page was in the old tongue. You should not have been
able to read it. You read it. The body and the kingdom have been teaching you, slowly.
The paragraph said something important. You will, in some other run, write it back into a margin.
(+1 Attack, permanently — an old-tongue paragraph you read without realising you could.)
Tonight the book, untouched on the table, breathed open — a slight, decided motion — at
the exact page you needed. You did not, in the end, ask how. You read the page. The
book closed when you closed it. The book will do this for you again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a book that has begun to open to the right page for you.)
Tonight your thumb came away from a page with a small dark smudge — the kingdom's old
soot-ink, the kind the rite was written in. The smudge will not wash off. You will
carry, on your right thumb, a small mark of the rite-ink from now on. You can carry it.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — soot-ink from the rite on your thumb that will not wash away.)
Tonight the book ended one chapter and the next page was blank — pen and ink set next
to it. The kingdom was waiting. You picked up the pen. You wrote a short sentence. You
closed the book. The next reader will read your sentence. The kingdom keeps the chain.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a sentence you wrote into a discovery-book for the next reader.)
Tonight you noticed a page had been torn out of the book — clean, careful. The kingdom
tears pages for safety. The page that was here held something the kingdom decided
should not, today, be known. You did not press. The torn page is somewhere safe.
Tonight a page in the book had been folded down at the corner — by a hand other than
yours, the kingdom's hand-of-recommendation. You read that page. It was, indeed, the
one you needed. You unfolded the corner before you closed the book. The kingdom does not need credit.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a dog-eared page in a book left for you by a hand that knew.)
Tonight on a page there was a diagram — a small one, a chart of stars, perhaps. When
you looked again, the diagram had shifted half a finger to the right on the page. The
diagram has decided about you. The diagram is, in some way, alive. You closed the book carefully.
Tonight a page in the book smelled of a kitchen — onions, broth, the kingdom's old
cooking. The reader before you had been a cook. They had read the book at a kitchen
table. You read the page. You felt fed by it. The kitchen-smell will be on you a while.
Tonight the book — small, ordinary by its size — was, in your hands, very heavy. The
weight of what was in it. You could feel the weight. You read the book. You will be
tireder, in your arms, for having held it. The weight will not, properly, leave you.
(-1 Max Stamina, permanently — a book too heavy in your hands for what it weighed by size.)
Tonight at a chapter break in the book there was a small glyph — one of the kingdom's
old marks — that had not, on previous readings, been there. The glyph was for you. The
glyph meant *keep going*. You did, in the reading. You will, in the rest of the climb.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a chapter-break glyph of *keep going* placed in the book for you.)
Tonight tucked into a page there was a small lock of hair — light, fine, a child's. The
page was the kind of page where a parent might press such a thing. You did not, in the
end, take the lock. You closed it back into the book. The child is somewhere they are.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a lock of child's hair pressed into a page that you closed back.)
Tonight a paragraph in the book was — by its language, by its small specific kindness —
addressed to you. Not by name. By the shape of who you are. The kingdom had written you
a letter and folded it into a discovery. You read it. You stood up steady. You stood up known.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a paragraph addressed to the shape of you, in a discovery-book.)
Tonight you turned the last page of the book and the signature at the end was yours. A
previous you's. You had read your own writing all the way through, not knowing. The
book is, in this way, a letter from a previous you to this you. You will, in some other run, write the next.
Tonight at the back of the book there was an index — by topic — that did not, on
previous readings, exist. Someone had compiled it for the next reader. The next reader
was you. The index is short. The entries are exactly the things you needed to look up.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — an index someone compiled at the back of a book just for the next reader's eye.)
Tonight, finishing the book, you had the distinct sense — for one count — that the
book had thanked you for reading it. Not the author. The book itself. Books, in
Mournhold, last a long time. They learn to be glad of readers. This book was glad of you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a book whose own thanks landed in you at the closing of its last page.)
Tonight Halna at her stall had cut a piece of meat to your specific size — your hands
open wide, the kingdom's old measure. She knows your hands. The cut is yours. The cut
fits. You took it. You ate it. You ate exactly enough. She had been watching your eating.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a cut Halna sized to your hands without your asking.)
Tonight Halna, in passing, called you *kid* — the way she would call her own. She did
not correct herself. She did not, you think, notice she had. You did. You did not say.
Tonight Halna let you help unload — the morning carts, the carcasses, the dry work.
She does not let anyone. You carried two sides of mutton in for her. Your shoulders
ached. They ache the right way. You are, in some small kingdom-old sense, of her household.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — shoulder-strength a butcher's daughter taught you to use.)
Tonight Halna, between customers, told you the name of her sister. The one who didn't
come back. She had not said the name to anyone in a long time. You said the name back.
She nodded once. Did not look at you for the count it took to settle. You both kept the count.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — Halna's sister's name said aloud between you twice.)
Tonight Halna handed you an apron — old, stained the colour of dried blood, the kind
the kingdom's old butchers wore. *Yours,* she said. You did not protest. You put it on.
You wore it the whole day at her stall. You take it with you now. Folded, in your pack.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a butcher's old apron Halna handed you and called yours.)
Tonight at Helka's bench she taught you, sitting beside you, how to read the river. The
colours of it. Which mean shallow, which mean a hidden bench, which mean the kingdom's
old drowned still under. You will read the river from now on. Your eye will know.
(+1 Defense, permanently — Helka's river-reading lesson at the bench, kept by your eye.)
Tonight Helka, between counts of looking at the water, handed you a small bronze
buckle she had made — bone-and-bronze, the kingdom's old way, the kind you cannot buy
anywhere. You put it on your belt. The buckle holds. The buckle will not break.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a bone-and-bronze buckle Helka made you that does not break.)
Tonight Helka told you what *drowned* means in the kingdom's old tongue — not what your
tongue means, but what hers. The word is older. The word means *given back to the water.*
Not lost. Given back. The Reach is full of given-back people. You will think of them differently.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — Helka's old-tongue meaning of *drowned* kept whole in you.)
Tonight Helka, for the first time, did not sit on her bench. *You take it tonight,*
she said. She had work elsewhere. You sat the bench out. The bench was hers and now,
tonight, was yours. You read the water as she had taught you. You stood up. The bench is yours.
(+1 Defense, permanently — Helka's bench given to you for one evening to sit alone.)
Tonight Helka, from her bench, called you over — pointed at a particular shape in the
water. *Someone is going back,* she said. The kingdom's old way of saying. You both
watched the going-back. You did not speak. You stood together. The water carried them along.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a going-back you witnessed with Helka at the bench, in silence.)
Tonight the lampkeeper, at dusk, handed you the lighter and let you light one of the
Crossroads lamps. The first one. You lit it. The flame caught. The lamp will, from
now on, lean toward you when you arrive. You have, in a small way, joined the lamps.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Crossroads lamp you lit at the lampkeeper's giving of the light.)
Tonight the lampkeeper, walking the row with you, told you which of the Crossroads
lamps remembers which old climber. Each lamp keeps one. You walked the row. You learned
the assignments. You will, in passing, salute the lamps for the climbers they hold.
Tonight the lampkeeper handed you a small flask of lamp-oil — the kingdom's old kind,
the slow-burning kind. *For the bad night,* she said. The flask is in your pack. The
oil will, when you need it, light you a lamp where no lamp had been. You will not waste it.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a flask of slow lamp-oil the lampkeeper gave you for the bad night.)
Tonight the lampkeeper, lighting the last lamp of the row, told you her first name. Not
*lampkeeper.* The one her mother used. You said it back. She blinked. *Yes,* she said.
You will use it sparingly. You will use it when you say goodnight, the last time you come to the row.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the lampkeeper's first name, given to you in trust.)
Tonight, leaving the Crossroads late, the lampkeeper walked you back along the lamp-row
until the next zone. She did not need to. She did. You walked under the lit lamps. The
kingdom's old escort. You said thank you at the end. She nodded. The next escort will be quieter.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a late-count escort along the lamp-row by the lampkeeper.)
Tonight the Survivor, who has fought the Pall longer than you, let you sit at her
table. She does not let anyone. You sat. You did not speak. She did not speak. You both
ate. The eating-together at her table is, in the kingdom, an old gesture of acknowledged kin.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Survivor's table-acceptance, the once.)
Tonight the Survivor, between drinks, taught you the small bracing — a tightening of
the chin, a planting of the boot — that the Pall does not, properly, get past. She has
kept herself sane with it for years. You will brace this way from now on. The Pall will note it.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Pall-bracing manner taught by the only one who outlasted it as long.)
Tonight the Survivor, after a long count of silence, named her husband. The one the
Pall took twenty years ago. She said it once. The name held in the air a count. Then
she did not say it again. You will not say it. You will carry it. So will she.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — the Survivor's husband's name held in the air between you the once.)
Tonight the Survivor took down, from above her fire, the old blade she had carried in
her own climbing. She let you handle it. You felt its weight. You handed it back. She
smiled. *It would still cut for you,* she said. The blade chose you, briefly. You stood straighter.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the Survivor's old blade weighed in your hand, briefly chosen by it.)
Tonight Tálva let you into the inner room of the Hidden Hold — the room with the names
of the starved on its walls. You walked the wall. You said one of the names aloud. Tálva
did not interrupt. You walked back out. You will be welcome in the room from now on.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Hidden Hold's inner room opened to you by Tálva's hand.)
Tonight Tálva told you about the winter — the famine winter, the one the capital sealed
the gates for. She told it slow. She told the parts the kingdom does not, anymore, want
told. You listened the whole count. The winter is in you now. You will carry it forward.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the famine-winter as Tálva told it, kept whole in you.)
Tonight Tálva, before you left, pressed a small loaf into your hand. *For the Reckoning,*
she said. *Give it to whoever needs it most.* You took the bread. It rides in your pack.
You will know who when you see them. You will give the bread. You will not eat it yourself.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a Reckoning-bread Tálva pressed into your hand for someone else.)
Tonight Tálva, in passing, called you *kin*. Not friend. Not climber. Kin. The kingdom's
old word, the heavy one, the one that means *we are of the same starving.* You did not
correct her. You stood up at the word. You will carry it. She nodded. She had decided.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — Tálva's *kin* word given to you, the heaviest of the kingdom's bonds.)
Tonight Tálva taught you the count — the exact count — of the starved at the gates of
the capital that winter. The number is in you now. It is a small heavy thing. You will,
when the Reckoning comes, say the number. The number will, when said, take.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the exact count of the famine-winter starved, kept in you for the Reckoning.)
Tonight Tálva sat with you in silence for a long count. Neither of you spoke. The hold
around you was quiet. The kingdom was quiet. You both kept the silence as if it were
a small prayer. It was. You will, from now on, hold silences longer. So will she.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a long silence held with Tálva that taught your lungs patience.)
Tonight Cael — through the wall — taught you to speak through dust as well as through
stone. The kingdom's older priests did this. You spoke a sentence into the dust at the
third wall. The dust held it. The dust will hold what you say to it from now on.
(+1 Attack, permanently — Cael's speak-through-dust lesson, kept by your throat.)
Tonight Cael — through the wall — said you could lay your palm on the old seal. The
seal that nobody lays a palm on. You laid your palm. The seal was warm. The seal had
been waiting for the next hand. You did not press it. You took your palm back. The warmth stayed.
(+1 Defense, permanently — your palm on the old seal of the kingdom, briefly, by Cael's permission.)
Tonight Cael told you, through the stone, the name of the priest in the third wall. The
priest who had refused the rite. The name was new to you. You said the name back through
the stone. The stone took it. The priest, you think, was glad. You both stood quiet.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the third-wall priest's name said back through stone by you.)
Tonight Cael, before you left, thanked you. *For coming. Again. Not all of them come
again.* The kingdom's old courtesy. You said *of course.* Cael, through the stone, was
quiet a count. You both kept the quiet. You will come back again. You both know.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — Cael's thanks for coming back, given through stone in the old way.)
Tonight Atrél, at the end of your visit, let you blow out the altar candle. Nobody blows
out Atrél's candle. You blew. The candle went. The smoke went up in a thin straight line.
Atrél thanked you with a tilt of their head. The candle will be lit, by you, next visit.
(+1 Defense, permanently — Atrél's candle blown out by your breath, the once.)
Tonight Atrél taught you the *un-kneel* — the way the kingdom's old priests stand up
from a long kneeling without losing the steadiness of the kneel. You stood up the new
way. Your knees thanked you. You will, from now on, stand up steadier from your sittings.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — Atrél's un-kneel taught your knees a steadier rising.)
Tonight, leaving the altar, you saw — at the path's first bend — a small lantern. Lit.
Atrél had walked, after you left, to set it for you. You walked back to thank them.
They had already returned to the altar. You walked the lit path. The lantern was you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a lantern Atrél set on your walking-path between altar and the road.)
Tonight the hearth-keeper had set a chair — yours, the one with the worn arm — by the
inn's fire. The exact arc that would catch the heat best. You sat. The fire warmed you
for the night. You will, from now on, sit in that chair. The chair has decided about you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a chair set in the best arc of the fire by the hearth-keeper's hand.)
Tonight in the morning, the hearth-keeper had — even though you slept late — kept
breakfast for you under a cloth. The eggs were still warm. The bread was buttered. She
did not, in the end, charge you for the lateness. *Eat,* she said. You ate. You went on.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a breakfast kept warm for your late waking by the inn-keeper.)
Tonight the hearth-keeper told you about the winter she had at Gravewatch when she was
small — long before the Pall, before the rite. The town had been a town. Children had
played in the square. You listened. The town's older life is in you now. You stood up easier.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the hearth-keeper's old-Gravewatch winter, given to you in a telling.)
Tonight the hearth-keeper, between counts of looking at you — bruised, tired — did not
take your coin. *On the house,* she said. *Tonight only.* You did not protest. You slept
without paying for the bed. The bed was warmer for the not-paying. You will, in some way, repay.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a free night at the inn given to you out of the hearth-keeper's eye for tiredness.)
Tonight the Reader — who watches back, who reads — loaned you a small book from their
stack. *Bring it back when you can,* they said. *Or don't.* You took it. You read it
between zones. You will, in fact, bring it back. The Reader will be glad of the bringing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a book loaned to you by the Reader without security of return.)
Tonight the Reader read aloud to you for an hour — a passage they had wanted to share,
a thing they thought you would want to hear. You sat through the whole hour. You did
not speak. The Reader closed the book. You both kept the silence after. You stood up steadier.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — an hour of being read aloud to by the Reader, kept in you.)
Tonight the Reader corrected your pronunciation of an old-tongue word you had used. Not
unkindly. *Like this,* they said. You said it again. The new way was right. The word
will, from now on, be said the way the Reader said it. You will sound less of an outsider.
(+1 Attack, permanently — an old-tongue pronunciation the Reader corrected for you, kept.)
Tonight the Reader told you what they had been reading all month. The Reader does not,
properly, say. They said tonight. You listened. The book was old, the topic specific.
You said you would, when you could, read it too. They smiled. They knew you would not.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Reader telling you what they read, the once, between counts of silence.)
Tonight reading in a discovery you saw, in the margin, the Reader's hand — small, neat,
the small Y at the foot of every comment. They had been here before you. They had left
their notes for the next reader. The next reader was you. You read the margin first.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Reader's marginalia in a discovery-book left for the next reader.)
Tonight the Reader, mid-page, let a single tear fall onto the book. They did not wipe
it. They did not, in the end, hide it from you. You did not, in the end, look away. The
Reader has decided you are someone in front of whom they can, briefly, cry. You stood with them.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — the Reader allowing themselves, in front of you, to cry at a page.)
Tonight the Insomniac let you walk Gravewatch's third-watch with him. The streets at
the dead hour. You walked the whole route. You did not speak. He did not speak. The
town kept its quiet. You stood up easier afterwards. You will walk with him again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a third-watch walk shared with the Insomniac in proper silence.)
Tonight the Insomniac told you what he counts — the small things he keeps numbered to
keep himself in the world. He counts the lamps. He counts the doorposts. He counts the
cats. You will, you suspect, start counting things too. The counting will help. You will count.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Insomniac's counting-method taught to you for the bad nights.)
Tonight at the third hour, awake at the saving-place, you came down to the inn's empty
room and the Insomniac had — without saying — kept a chair across from him. You sat. He
did not look up from his book. You sat the count out. You both, in your way, slept after.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a chair kept across from the Insomniac for whoever could not sleep.)
Tonight the Insomniac, finding you cold at the saving-hour, handed you the small thin
blanket he carries with him on his walks. *I don't sleep,* he said. *You should.* You
took the blanket. You slept under it. It is in your pack now. You will give it back, eventually.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Insomniac's walking-blanket given to you for the night.)
Tonight the Insomniac, parting on the corner before dawn, called you *fellow walker.* The
kingdom's old name for someone who keeps watch through the bad hours. He had decided
you were one. You did not protest. You will, you suspect, keep more watches now. So will he.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Insomniac's *fellow walker* given to you in the kingdom's old usage.)
Tonight the smith — Halna's daughter — let you swing her hammer once at the anvil. Just
one strike. She watched the strike. *Better than I thought,* she said. The hammer rang
the right ring. Your hand will remember the swing. You will, from now on, swing truer.
(+1 Attack, permanently — one strike at the smith's anvil that the smith decided was good.)
Tonight the smith told you about the first blade she ever made — the small one, the
child's knife, the one her mother kept. The blade is in Halna's stall, still. You had
not noticed. You will look for it next time. Halna will not, you think, mind that you know.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the smith's first-blade story and where it now lives, kept by you.)
Tonight the smith, between counts of the forge, handed you a small leather pouch with
two horseshoe nails in it. *For luck,* she said. The kingdom's old way. You took the
pouch. The nails are in your pocket. They will, in the bad hour, be there to hold.
(+1 Defense, permanently — two horseshoe nails the smith gave you for luck in the kingdom's old way.)
Tonight at the Karst Outpost — long abandoned — the lookout-post had, since your last
visit, accumulated a small shelf of pebbles. Travellers leave them in the kingdom's old
way to mark *I was here.* The shelf had recognized your previous pebble. It welcomed you.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Karst lookout-shelf welcoming you back with the old pebble-mark.)
Tonight at the Karst Outpost the small kettle by the empty hearth was, when you put
your palm to it, warm. Recently. There had been somebody. They had gone. They had not
wanted to be seen. The kingdom's borderlands keep their walkers. You walked through.
Tonight at the Karst Outpost the signpost had, on its fourth arm, the name of a town
you used to live in. The town does not exist anymore. The signpost still points. You
did not walk that way. You walked on. The signpost will keep pointing, for the next walker.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a signpost arm pointing to a town that does not exist anymore.)
Tonight at the Karst you found, in the dust, a small bronze token — an emissary's mark
from one of the sister realms. The realm's name on it is one the kingdom has begun to
forget. You took the token. It rides in your pocket. The realm will know its mark is with someone.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a sister realm's emissary-token kept in your pocket against forgetting.)
Tonight at the Karst a bird — large, wings spread wide — circled three times over the
outpost as you arrived. The kingdom's old way of marking a particular visitor. The bird
went. You looked after it. The next traveller will know to expect you, on some count.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a three-circling bird marking your arrival at the Karst in the old way.)
Tonight at the Karst there was, at the empty table, a chair set facing yours. You sat
in your chair. The opposite chair stayed empty. You both — you and the empty chair —
kept the count. The Karst, by its old courtesy, had set you a conversation-partner.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a Karst chair set facing yours that kept its count empty in your honour.)
Tonight at Wynne's camp the cooking fire — long cold, you would have thought — had a
single warm coal at the bottom. You blew. The coal caught. You had a small fire by the
time you needed it. Wynne would have been glad. The kingdom kept the coal for you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a single warm coal at Wynne's camp the kingdom kept against your need.)
Tonight at Wynne's the central post had — newly, since you were last here — a small
carved message: *the road south is still open.* You did not need the road south. You
noted it. You will, in some future, need it. The carver will be glad of the saving.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a carved message at Wynne's about the still-open south road.)
Tonight at Wynne's, between counts, you heard — distantly, on the wind from the south —
a song you almost knew. The sister realm sings it. You stood listening until the wind shifted. The song was gone.
Tonight at Wynne's you noticed, in the corner where someone had clearly slept, a small
carving you recognized. A friend's mark. They had camped here once. Years ago. You did
not know they had come this far. They had. You knelt at the corner. You said their name.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a friend's old camp-mark at Wynne's that you recognized and named.)
Tonight at Wynne's the central post had a small arrowhead lodged in it — not the
kingdom's make. A sister realm's. You worked it out with your knife. You took it. It
rides in your pocket. The arrowhead remembers a hand that drew the bow that put it there.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a sister realm's arrowhead worked from Wynne's post into your pocket.)
Tonight at Wynne's, tucked under a stone, there was a small folded letter — to you, by
name. From an emissary you had spoken with three runs ago. *We watch your road,* it
said. *Walk well.* You folded it. You kept it. The emissary is in this with you, still.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a sister-realm emissary's letter to you, kept in your pack.)
Tonight at the Margrave's monument the old seal — the carved one, the kingdom's old
writ — was warm under your palm. The seal had been keeping itself warm. The seal had
decided about you. The writ, in the seal's memory, is, for one count, honored again.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Margrave's seal warm under your palm, honoring an old writ.)
Tonight at the monument there was — folded, tucked into the wreath — a petition. The
old form. Addressed *to the next who comes.* You read it. You did not have the power
to grant. You did not have the power to refuse. You read it. The reading was, in itself, the answer.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a petition left at the Margrave's monument for whoever next came; you read it.)
Tonight at the monument, kneeling, you felt — for one count — a hand on your shoulder.
Old. Heavy. The kind of hand that knows the weight of a writ. The dead Margrave was,
briefly, with you. The hand thanked you. You did not turn. The hand withdrew. You stood.
Tonight at the monument the stone where you knelt — between the dais and the wreath —
had, by the time you stood, softened slightly. The stone has been kneeling-stone for a
long count. It has, in this run, decided you are kin to its kneelers. The stone remembers.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a kneeling-stone at the Margrave's monument that softened for you.)
Tonight at the monument, in the wreath of withered flowers, you found a small folded
paper. Old. The Margrave's writ — one of the originals. Faded. You read it. You did not
leave it back. The writ rides in your pocket. The kingdom may, one day, need it produced.
(+1 Attack, permanently — an original Margrave's writ kept in your pocket against the kingdom's need.)
Tonight leaving the monument, the bell — the small one, the kind that rings when a
writ-bearer departs — rang once. There was no one to ring it. The bell rang for you.
The Margrave's monument has decided you are, in some sense it kept, a bearer. You walked.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Margrave's bell rung once at your leaving by no hand.)
Tonight you killed a Hollowed who had, you understood late, been somebody. The kill
did not settle well. You laid them out. You said the small words. The kingdom thanked
you. You did not feel thanked. You will feel less thanked from now on by such kills.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a Hollowed kill that did not, in the end, settle in you well.)
Tonight, after killing a Hollowed, you knelt and took one button from their coat. The
kingdom's old courtesy — to remember the dead by something they had worn. The button
rides in your pocket. You will, when you can, give it to someone who knew them.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Hollowed's coat-button kept by you to give back, when you can, to a knower.)
Tonight, after the Hollowed went down, you saw the face under the hollow. You knew
the face. A neighbour from before the kingdom. Not someone you'd ever spoken to. Someone
you had passed every day. You closed their eyes. You walked on. The face will stay.
(-2 Max HP, permanently — a familiar face under a Hollowed's hollow that you, tonight, recognized.)
Mid-fight tonight a Hollowed's breath synced with yours — for three counts. The count
they breathe in. You learned it. You can, from now on, breathe in their count. The
Hollowed will, when you do, be less hostile to you. You will, when you do, be calmer.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Hollowed's breath-count taught to your lungs in a fight.)
Tonight in the worst beat the Hollowed called you a name — not yours. The name was
someone they had loved. You finished the fight. You laid them out. You said the name
they had called you, back to them, before walking on. The kingdom may have heard you.
Tonight you walked past a Hollowed humming the kingdom's old funeral-tune. They lowered
their hand. They let you pass. The funeral-tune is the kingdom's old way of saying *I
have already laid one of yours out.* You had. The Hollowed had decided to honor it.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a Hollowed letting you pass because you were humming their old tune.)
Tonight, mid-fight, you traded a blow for a word — landed the strike but said, in the
landing, the kingdom's old word for *forgive.* The Hollowed went quieter. The fight
shorter. You can, you understand, trade blows for words. The kingdom honours the trade.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a blow you landed that you accompanied with the word *forgive.*)
Tonight when the Hollowed went down their hands, instead of curling, opened. Palms up.
The kingdom's old gesture of *I have nothing left to take.* You closed their eyes. You
did not close the hands. You let the palms stay up. The kingdom saw. The kingdom kept the gesture.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a Hollowed's open palms at death that you let stay up.)
Tonight the Hollowed you killed had — by the size of their hands, the smallness of the
feet — been a child once. Not now. Their body had grown. The Hollow had kept the child-
sized hands. You did not, in the end, eat that night. You stood the long count out.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a Hollowed whose child-sized hands you noticed only after laying them out.)
Tonight close-fighting a Hollowed, their breath against your face was — for one count —
warmer than yours. The Hollow had something living in it still. You did not, in the
end, kill them. You sidestepped. They left. You stood. The kingdom does not, always, want killing.
Tonight under the hood of the Hollowed you laid out was a friend. An old one. Your
own. You did not, before tonight, know they had fallen. You did not, before tonight,
know they had risen. You sat with the body the long count. You said the name. You went on.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — an old friend whose Hollow-rising you discovered tonight by killing them.)
Tonight the Hollowed, as they died, sang — softly, in the kingdom's old tongue — a
lullaby. They had been, before being Hollowed, a parent. The lullaby was theirs. They
finished the verse before they went. You knew the verse. You could not, in the end, sleep.
(-1 Max HP, permanently — a Hollowed's lullaby sung at their dying that took some of your sleep.)
Tonight the Hollowed you killed was carrying — under their coat — a small book. You
took it. You opened it. It was theirs, from when they were a person. They had loved
the book. You will read it. You will, in some way, keep loving it for them.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a Hollowed's book taken from their body to read and keep for them.)
Tonight when you finished the laying-out — palms folded, eyes closed, the small words —
the kingdom around you breathed out. You felt the breathing-out. It was, for the kingdom,
a small relief. You did, in this one, the work properly. The kingdom thanked you in air.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the kingdom breathing out at one of your properly-laid Hollowed.)
Tonight a Hollowed walked beside you for a short count — not threatening. As an old
companion does. Did not speak. Did not, properly, exist. You walked together. Then it
lifted a hand and went. The Hollowed had decided to escort you for one stretch of road.
Tonight in Gravewatch's market a vendor — you had not, before, particularly spoken to
— called your usual order as you approached. *Two of the dark loaves, no?* You said
yes. The vendor had noted what you buy. You have a usual order. You are part of the market.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a market vendor who has memorized your order without your asking.)
Tonight in Gravewatch a kettle — somewhere, on someone's hearth — whistled as you
walked past their door. The kingdom is still doing its small daily things. You walked
on warmed by the sound. You will, you suspect, set a kettle of your own going one of these nights.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a kettle's whistle on someone's hearth that warmed you in passing.)
Tonight at the saving, before dawn, you sat at the window and the dawn came in clean.
Cleaner than it has been since you came. The kingdom is still capable of clean dawns.
You stood up under the dawn. You will, you suspect, look at more dawns from now on.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a clean dawn watched at Gravewatch's window in proper attention.)
Tonight in Gravewatch a dog you have walked past many times — old, half-blind, the kind
that sleeps in the second alley — barked. Not in alarm. In recognition. The dog has
decided you are friend. You crouched. You scratched the ridge of fur. You said good-dog.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a Gravewatch dog's friendly recognition of your step.)
Tonight in the market a vendor was singing the kingdom's old song — the one from before
the rite, the one that was supposed to be forgotten. The song is, in small ways, coming
back into the market. The kingdom is, in small ways, remembering. You walked listening.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the kingdom's old song sung in the market by a vendor who knew it.)
Tonight on the second bench in Gravewatch's square an old one shifted over and patted
the empty space beside them. The kingdom's old way. You sat. You did not speak. They
did not speak. You both watched the square. They stood up first. The seat is, now, yours.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a seat on the Gravewatch bench offered to you in the old way.)
Tonight you saw the Gravewatch baker, at the back of the shop, scattering yesterday's
bread for the birds. The kingdom keeps small kindnesses. You stood at the corner
watching. The baker saw. They scattered a little extra. The kingdom feeds itself in pieces.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a baker's scattering for birds in the kingdom's small mercies.)
Tonight in the market a vendor and a customer haggled — long, in the kingdom's old
ritual — and the haggle ended in laughter. Honest laughter. The kingdom is, today,
capable of laughter. You watched the laughing. You did not, in the end, buy anything yourself.
Tonight at the market a stranger handed you a length of rope to help with a load you
were carrying. *Bring it back when you can,* they said. You used it. You brought it back.
The stranger smiled. You will, in some future, do the same for someone else's load.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a rope loaned by a stranger for carrying, returned with thanks.)
Tonight a chimney in Gravewatch — long unused, the house empty — had been swept. The
soot was on the cobbles. Someone had decided. The empty house may have someone in it
by week's end. The kingdom is filling back in by small acts. You walked on heartened.
Tonight in a small front garden in Gravewatch a neighbour was planting a bulb. The
kingdom plants bulbs again. The bulb will come up in spring. You will not, in this
run, see it. You will, you tell yourself, come back for the bloom. The kingdom is hoping again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a bulb planted in front of a Gravewatch house that will bloom after you.)
Tonight a window in Gravewatch — the kind that has been closed for fear since the Pall —
was open. The keeper was, briefly, letting in air. The air on the cobbles smelled, for a
count, of indoors. The kingdom is, in small ways, opening back up. You stood and breathed.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a window opened to let in air, the kingdom's small reopening.)
Tonight in Gravewatch a door — a house you had not, before, particularly noticed — was
left unlocked. The keeper had not pulled it shut behind them. The neighbours had not,
you can see, alarmed about it. The kingdom is trusting again. Doors are, again, doors.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a door left unlocked in Gravewatch in the kingdom's returning trust.)
Tonight in Gravewatch's back yard a clothes-line was up — pinned with washing. The
kingdom is washing again. The wet linen drips on the cobbles. The smell of clean soap.
You walked under the line. The shape of clean shirts above you. The kingdom is doing its life.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a clothes-line strung in a Gravewatch back yard with clean washing on it.)
Tonight in the alley a child was throwing a stick for a dog. The kingdom plays again.
The child was small. The dog was old. They were a complete world for the count of the
playing. You walked through their game on the cobbles. They did not, properly, notice you.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a child and a dog at stick-throwing in a Gravewatch alley.)
Tonight a child in Gravewatch's square offered you a pebble. Particular. Polished. The
kingdom's children are precise about their gifts. You took the pebble. You thanked them
properly. The pebble rides in your pocket. The child is, somewhere, holding onto the giving.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a pebble a child gave you, precise about the giving.)
Tonight two children were playing a game on the cobbles — clapping hands, chanting,
the rhyme the kingdom's children used to chant. You knew the rhyme. You had played it.
You stood watching. They did not see you. The rhyme is coming back. The kingdom remembers.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — the kingdom's children's clapping rhyme that you remembered watching.)
Tonight a child taught you — solemnly, carefully — a new word they had invented. The
word means *cold-feeling-warm.* You said it back. They corrected your pronunciation.
You learned the word properly. The word is yours now too. The kingdom's children make new words.
(+1 Attack, permanently — a child-invented word for *cold-feeling-warm* taught to you carefully.)
Tonight a child — small, the kind that walks up to strangers without asking — sat on
the bench next to you for a long count. They did not speak. You did not speak. You
watched the square together. They got up and ran off. The kingdom's small acceptance.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a child's small unspoken sit-beside-you, the once.)
Tonight a child asked, frankly, about the scar on your hand. *Where did you get it?*
You did not, in the end, say. You said *climbing.* The child accepted *climbing.* They
had wanted, you think, an honest answer. You will, when they are older, tell them honestly.
Tonight at the inn, slipped under your door, a folded piece of paper. A child's drawing.
You. With your blade. Standing on the Crossroads. The drawing was careful. The drawing
was hopeful. You folded it carefully into your pack. You will not, in this run, lose it.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a child's drawing of you with your blade, folded into your pack.)
Tonight a child sang — quietly, to themselves, on a doorstep — the kingdom's old
lullaby. The Pall took the lullabies. This child has one back. You stood at the corner
listening to the end of the verse. The child did not see you. The lullaby took. The kingdom is healing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the kingdom's old lullaby sung by a child on a doorstep, returning.)
