Tonight at the inn, the table being full, a child stood up — without being asked — and
let you have their seat. The kingdom's old courtesy. You did not, in the end, take it.
You sat on the floor. The child sat on your knee. You ate together. The inn was full of laughing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a child standing to give you a seat that you ended up sharing on the floor.)
Tonight at the inn a child pointed, out the window, at one of the brighter stars. *That
one is yours,* they said. They had decided. The kingdom's children sometimes decide such
things. The star is yours. You said *thank you* to the child. The star will be yours always.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a star a child decided was yours, named for you against the kingdom's sky.)
Tonight in the square a child — sent on an errand, you think — handed you a small warm
pastry. *Mother said,* they said. You ate it. It was good. You said thank-you to the
child for the mother. The child ran off. The mother, somewhere, smiled when the child returned.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a warm pastry handed to you by a child sent on an errand.)
Tonight at the Crossroads a child — they were going to Gravewatch with a small basket —
walked the first part of your path with you. They asked you which star was yours. You
showed them. They nodded approval. They split off at the gate. The road will keep them safe.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a child who walked part of your path and asked which star was yours.)
Tonight a child, watching you sharpen your blade on the inn's bench, said quietly, *I
am going to climb too, when I am bigger.* They were small. They were serious. You did
not, in the end, tell them what the climb was. You said *good.* They smiled. They will.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — a child's promise to climb when they are bigger, said in seriousness.)
Tonight, walking between zones, you passed a milestone that — when your foot landed
next to it — gave, faintly, a thrum through the stone. The milestone recognized your
step. The road has begun to know you, foot by foot. The other milestones will follow.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a milestone between zones that thrummed at your step in recognition.)
Tonight on the road between zones a traveller passed you the other way. You nodded.
They nodded. Neither of you spoke. The kingdom's old courtesy of the road. You did not
see their face under the hood. They did not see yours. The nod was the whole thing.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a silent nod exchanged with a hooded traveller on the road between zones.)
Tonight on the road there was a small wayside shrine — Atrél's, of the road, the old
wayside one. You did not stop. The ground a pace before the shrine warmed under your
boot anyway. Atrél, even at the wayside, knew you were passing. You walked on heartened.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a wayside Atrél-shrine warming the road for your step in passing.)
Tonight a stretch of road between zones — one you have walked many times — was, by
your reckoning, shorter than it has been. Not the road. You. Your body has, over the
run, become a better walker of it. From now on, every mile of the kingdom is, for you, shorter.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a road become shorter under feet that have walked it many times.)
Tonight on the road you found an old horseshoe — half-buried, the iron rusted, the
kingdom's old kind. You pried it out. You nailed it, that night, above the inn-door at
your next stop. The innkeeper saw. She did not, in the end, take it down. It is there.
(+1 Defense, permanently — an old horseshoe nailed above an inn-door by your hand, kingdom-style.)
Tonight crossing a bridge between zones the stream below — the small one, the kind
that the kingdom keeps fed with rain — gave a faint sound. Thanks, in its way. You had,
in some run, helped clear a snag from its bend. The stream remembered. You walked on.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a small stream under a bridge thanking you for an old kindness you don't remember.)
Tonight on the road you noticed, looking back, that your footprint in the dust had not
yet, in the count it usually does, blown out. The wind was strong. The print was still
there. The kingdom has decided to remember your particular step on this stretch. Just this stretch.
Tonight arriving at a wayside post at dusk the small lantern there came on as you
stepped past. There was no hand. The lantern, in the kingdom's old way, lit itself for
a walker who had been at it a long time. The lantern recognized your kind. You walked on lit.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a wayside lantern that lit itself at your dusk-arrival.)
Tonight, walking between zones, you found yourself humming the whole way — the kingdom's
old walking-song, the one the long-roads merchants used to use. The song carried you
the whole distance. You did not tire as much. The song will walk with you again.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the kingdom's old long-roads walking-song that carried you a whole distance.)
Tonight at dusk a blackbird — small, sharp-eyed — flew with you, branch to branch, for
the last half-hour of your walking. Lit your way, in the kingdom's old way of saying.
The kingdom's old birds did this for the kingdom's old travellers. You walked accompanied.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a blackbird that lit your way at dusk in the kingdom's old manner.)
Tonight the Pall — without your turning — set your foot facing the Summit. Not pushed.
Set. Your foot, when you next set it down, fell facing up the mountain. The Pall is
saying goodbye to the climber. The Pall is welcoming you to the role. You did not refuse.
(+1 Defense, permanently — the Pall setting your foot to face the Summit in welcome.)
Tonight at the saving-place the Pall, briefly, sat with you and said goodbye — through
no words — to the version of you who first came into Mournhold. That you is gone now.
The Pall thanked them on your behalf. You stood under the thanks. You will not be that you again.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — the Pall's farewell to the version of you who first walked in.)
Tonight the Pall — out of the small accounting it has been keeping — returned one of
your kindnesses to you. A particular one. The small thing you did for the widow on the
second street, that you had thought forgotten. The Pall returned it. The widow, somewhere, smiled.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a kindness of yours the Pall returned, with interest, in farewell.)
Tonight your breath, for a long count, synced — fully — with the Pall's. Inhale together.
Exhale together. You felt the kingdom breathe with the rhythm. The Pall does this only
for whom it has chosen. The rhythm has been chosen for. You walked on at the kingdom's pace.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a long-count breath-sync with the Pall, the kingdom's own breathing.)
Tonight at the saving-place the Pall, without speaking, taught you the first word of
the keeping — the word a Warden says, the first day, to begin the work. You will say
it when you arrive. Your throat already knows. The word will, when said, take properly.
(+1 Attack, permanently — the first word of the keeping that the Pall taught your throat.)
Tonight you found, in the morning, the first grey hair you got on this run — the one
the kingdom gave you back at the first level-up — laid neatly on your pillow. Not in
your hair anymore. Returned. The Pall took the grey back. The kingdom does not, anymore, need it.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — your first grey hair returned by the Pall, laid neatly on your pillow.)
Tonight at the saving-place the Pall let you hold, briefly, one of its memories. A child
in a kitchen, holding a small loaf, on the morning before the famine. You held the
memory for one count. You gave it back. The Pall took it back. You walked the rest changed.
(+3 Max HP, permanently — one of the Pall's own memories briefly held in your hand and returned.)
Tonight the Pall — looking at your companion at your side — made the carrying easier
for them. They walked taller after. They had not, you noticed, been tired in the count
you usually expected. The Pall did this as a small gift to a companion of its successor.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — the Pall easing your companion's carrying in farewell to your climbing-pair.)
Tonight the Pall, in your head, said your name and *Warden* together — for the first
time, aloud inside you. You did not protest. The name and the title together felt
correct. You will, when you arrive at the Summit, say the pairing aloud to the wind.
Tonight the Pall, in farewell, asked you to choose one thing it had taken from you in
the run — a face, a word, a kitchen-smell — and you could have it back. You chose. The
Pall returned it. The kingdom watched. The choosing is yours. The kept thing is yours, fully.
(+2 Max HP, permanently — one taken thing returned to you by the Pall at your choice, in farewell.)
Tonight, walking, a low hum — yours, almost without your noticing — carried you the
whole zone. The hum was the kingdom's old walking-hum. Your body learned it without
your asking. From now on, in the bad zones, you will hum it. It will carry you again.
(+1 Max Stamina, permanently — a low walking-hum the body learned that carries you across zones.)
Tonight at the saving-place you held a silence — long, particular, the right kind. The
silence landed correctly in the room. Some silences don't. This one did. You stood up
afterwards in the silence's shape. You will, from now on, hold the right silences in their right ways.
(+1 Defense, permanently — a silence held correctly at the saving, that the kingdom honoured.)
Tonight in the Choir the air was thinner — not by much, but noticeably. The kingdom's
old song was, in the wings, being half-sung by someone. You did not see by whom. The
song never arrived. You walked through the thinning. Air came back when you left.
Tonight at the inn the kettle on the hearth sang, in its small whistled way, a melody
that — by coincidence or design — was the kingdom's old hearth-song. You sat by the
kettle until it cooled. The hearth-song steadied something in you. You can carry it now.
(+1 Max HP, permanently — a kettle whistling the kingdom's old hearth-song that steadied something in you.)
Tonight an unsung verse of the kingdom's old Verse — one you had been carrying since
you found the lost line — came back, briefly, into the air around you. You sang it
under your breath. The verse took. The kingdom is, in pieces, getting back its songs.
(+1 Attack, permanently — an unsung verse of the kingdom's old Verse returned briefly into the air with you.)
Tonight you walked past the widow on the second bench in Gravewatch — the one who has
